questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{45}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{9}{20}{/tex} ={tex}\frac{9}{{20}}{/tex}

questions.question: S.P of a toy is ₹540. If the profit made by the shopkeeper is 20%, the C.P of the toy is ________.
questions.explanation:
S.P of a toy = ₹540
Let C.P = ₹100
After 20% profit, S.P = ₹ 100 + 20 = ₹120
Then, C.P is = {tex}\frac{540 \times 100}{120}{/tex} = ₹ 450

questions.question: Total numbers of beads in a bag are 20, if red beads are 8 and blue beads are 12, find out the percentage of blue colour of beads.
questions.explanation:
Total numbers of beads in a bag = 20
% of red beads = {tex}\frac{8}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 40 %
% of blue beads = {tex}\frac{12}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 60%

subtopics.name: Use of Percentages - Converting Percentages to "How Many" (VII-7.1)

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an article of ₹400, while he purchases it for ₹402. Find out loss percent of shopkeeper.
questions.explanation:
S.P of an article = ₹400
C.P of an article = ₹402
Loss = C.P - S.P = 402 - 400 = 2
Loss % = {tex}\frac{2}{402} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{201}{/tex} = 0.49%

questions.question: Rashmi buys a calculator for ₹720 and sells it at a loss of {tex}6\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex}. For how much does she sell it?
questions.explanation:
C. P of a calculator = ₹720
loss of {tex}6\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex} = {tex}720 \times \frac{20}{300}{/tex} = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 48
S.P of a calculator = C.P - Loss = ₹720 - ₹ 48 = ₹672

questions.question: The boys and girls in a school are in the ratio of 8 : 5. If the number of girls is 160, what is the total strength of the school?
questions.explanation:
Let total strength of school = X
No. of girls = {tex}(\frac{5}{8} + 5) \times X{/tex} = 160
{tex}\frac{5X}{13}{/tex} = 160
= 5X = 160 {tex}\times{/tex} 13
= X = 416
Thus, the total strength X = 416

questions.question: The simple interest on ₹5000 for 219 days at 4% per annum is
questions.explanation:
Total days in one year = 365 days
Simple Interest = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5000 \times 219 \times 4}{365 \times 100}{/tex}
= 120

questions.question: Find the simple interest on ₹2500 for 2 years 6 months at 6% per annum.
questions.explanation:
Time = 2 years 6 month = 2 years {tex}\frac{6}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
Simple Interest = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2500 \times 5 \times 6}{2 \times 100}{/tex}
= ₹375

questions.question: What sum will amount to ₹4590 at 12% per annum simple interest in 3 years?
questions.explanation:
Let the sum = ₹ P
Amount = Simple Interest + Principal
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{p \times 12 \times 3}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{36P}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{136P}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{4590 \times 100}{136}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = ₹3375

questions.question: In what time will ₹1860 amount to ₹2278.50, if simple interest is calculated at 9% per annum?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1860
Amount = ₹2278.50
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{1860 \times 9 \times T}{100}{/tex} + 1860
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{1674T}{10}{/tex} + 1860
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 - 1860 = 167.4T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 418.50 = 167.4T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T = {tex}\frac{418.5}{167.4}{/tex} = 2.5 = Means 2 years 6 month

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum will ₹1650 amount to ₹2046 in 3 years?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1650
Amount = ₹2046
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{1650 \times 3 \times R}{100}{/tex} + 1650
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{495R}{10}{/tex} + 1650
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 - 1650 = {tex}\frac{495R}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 396 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 495R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = {tex}\frac{3960}{495}{/tex} = 8%

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum simple interest will a sum treble itself in 16 years?
questions.explanation:
Let sum = P
Simple interest = 2P
S.I = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2P = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times 16}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 200P = 16PR
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{200}{16}{/tex} = R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 12.5%

questions.question: In what time will ₹1600 amount to ₹1768 at 6% per annum simple interest?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1600
Amount = ₹1768
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = {tex}\frac{1600 \times 6 \times T}{100}{/tex} + 1600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = 96T + 1600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768-1600 = 96T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 168 = 96T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T = {tex}\frac{168}{96}{/tex} = 1.75 = Means 1 years 9 month

questions.question: A sum amounts to ₹3720 in 8 months at 5% per annum simple interest. The sum is
questions.explanation:
Let Sum = P
Time = 8 month = {tex}\frac{8}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
Amount = ₹3720
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{P \times 5 \times 2}{3 \times 100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{P}{30}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{31P}{30}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{3720 \times 30}{31}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{111600}{31}{/tex} = ₹3600

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum simple interest will a sum be double itself in 8 years?
questions.explanation:
Let Principal = P
Then, S.I = P
Time = 8 years
Rate = ?
S.I = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
P = {tex}\frac{PR \times 8}{100}{/tex}
R = {tex}\frac{100P}{8P}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{8}{/tex} = 12.5%

questions.question: Mala has a collection of bangles. She has 20 gold bangles and 10 silver bangles. What is the percentage of bangles of each type?
questions.explanation:
Total bangles = 30
Percentage of gold bangles = {tex}\frac{20}{30} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{200}{3}{/tex} = 66.666 = 66%
Percentage of silver bangles = {tex}\frac{10}{30} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}{/tex} = 33.333 = 34%

questions.question: Rohan bought a second hand refrigerator for ₹2,500, then spent ₹500 on its repairs and sold it for ₹3,300. Find his loss or gain percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of a second hand refrigerator = ₹2,500
Spent Rs 500 on its repairs then the cost price = ₹2500 + 500 = ₹3000
Selling price of it = ₹3300
Gain = S.P - C.P = 3300 - 3000 = 300
Gain % = {tex}\frac{300}{3000} \times 100{/tex} = 10%

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 200 bulbs for ₹10 each. However, 5 bulbs were fused and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹12 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of 1 bulb = ₹10
Then, cost price of 200 bulbs = ₹200 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = ₹2000
5 bulbs were fused then, remaining bulbs = 200 - 5 = 195
Remaining were sold at ₹12 each then, Selling price of remaining bulbs = 195 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹2340
Gain = ₹2340 - 2000 = 340
Gain % = {tex}\frac{340}{2000} \times 100 {/tex} = 17%

questions.question: During a sale, a shop offered a discount of 10% on the marked prices of all the items. What would a customer have to pay for a pair of jeans marked at ₹1450 and two shirts marked at ₹850 each?
questions.explanation:
Cost of two shirts = ₹ 850 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = ₹1700
Customer have to pay for a pair of jeans and two shirts = 1450 - [1450 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{10}{100}{/tex}]
= 1450 - 145 = ₹1305
Customer have to pay for two shirts = 1700 - {2 {tex}\times{/tex} [850 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{10}{100}{/tex})]}
=1700 - {2 {tex}\times{/tex} 85}
=1700 - 170 = ₹1530
Total ₹ = 1305 + 1530 = ₹ 2,835

questions.question: An item marked at ₹840 is sold for ₹714. What is the discount %?
questions.explanation:
Marked price of an item = ₹840
Selling price = ₹714
Discount = ₹840 - 714 = ₹126
Discount% = {tex}\frac{126}{840} \times 100{/tex} = 15%

questions.question: The price of a car was ₹3,40,000 last year. It has increased by 20% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Last year, Cost price of a car = ₹3,40,000 
Increased by 20% this year = {tex}340000 \times \frac{20}{100}{/tex} = ₹68000
Now the price is = ₹340000 + 68000 = ₹4,08,000

questions.question: The price of a car was ₹5,00,000 last year. It has increased by 10% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Last year, Cost price of a car  = ₹5,00,000
Increased by 10% this year = ₹ {tex}500000 \times \frac{10}{100}{/tex} = ₹50000
Now, the price is = ₹ 500000 + 50000 = ₹5,50,000

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 300 bulbs for ₹10 each. However, 10 bulbs were fused and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹12 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
If the cost of 1 bulb = ₹10
Then, Cost of 300 bulbs = 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 3000 ₹
10 bulbs were fused then remaining = 300 - 10 = 290
Remaining were sold at ₹12 each then, selling price of remaining = 290 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹3480
Profit = S.P - C.P = 3480 - 3000 = 480 ₹
Profit % = {tex}\frac{480}{3000} \times 100{/tex} = 16%

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 500 pieces for ₹20 each. However 50 pieces were spoiled in the way and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹25 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
If the cost of 1 piece = ₹ 20
Then, Cost of 500 pieces = 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 10000 ₹
50 pieces were spoiled in the way then remaining = 500 - 50 = 450
Remaining were sold at ₹25 each then, selling price of remaining = 450 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = ₹ 11250
Profit = S.P - C.P = 11250 - 10000 = 1250 ₹
Profit % = {tex}\frac{1250}{10000} \times 100{/tex} = 12.5%

questions.question: The population of a city was 40,000 in the year 1999. It increased at a rate of 10% p.a. Find the population at the end of the year 2000.
questions.explanation:
10% of 40000 population = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 40000{/tex} = 4000
Population in 2000 = 40000 + 4000 = 44000

questions.question: 15% of 200 students are good in English. How many are not good in English?
questions.explanation:
Given 15% of students are good in English.
So, 100 - 15 = 85% of students are not good in English.

questions.question: Sheela bought two fans for ₹1200 each. She sold one at a loss of 5% and the other at a profit of 10%. Find the selling price of each. Also find out the total profit or loss.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of one fan= ₹1200
Let C.P of fan = ₹100
Loss of 5% on one fan then S.P. is ₹95
Then, S.P. of one fan = {tex}\frac{1200 \times 95}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 1140
Profit of 10% on second fan then S.P. is ₹110
Then, S.P. of second fan = ({tex}\frac{1200 \times 110}{100}{/tex}) = ₹1320
Total S.P = ₹. (1140 + 1320) = 2460 ₹
Total C.P = ₹ (1200 + 1200) = ₹ 2400
Profit = S.P - C.P = 2460 - 2400 = ₹ 60

questions.question: A milkman sold two of his buffaloes for ₹20,000 each. On one he made a gain of 5% and on the other a loss of 10%. Find his overall gain or loss.
questions.explanation:
Selling price of each buffalo = ₹20000
Then, C.P. of one buffalo with 5% profit = {tex}(20000\times100) \over 105{/tex} = ₹19047.62 {tex}[C.P={S.P.\times100 \over (100+profit \%)}]{/tex} 
Then, C.P. of second buffalo with 10% loss = {tex}(20000\times100) \over 90{/tex} = ₹ 22222.22 {tex}[C.P.={S.P.\times100 \over (100-loss \%)}]{/tex} 
Total C.P = ₹ (19047.62 + 22222.22) = 41269.84 ₹
Total S.P = ₹ (20000 + 20000) = ₹40000
Thus, overall Loss = C.P - S.P = 41269.84 - 40000 = ₹1269.84

questions.question: Out of 50 children in a class, 20 are boys. Then the percentage of girls is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac {50 - 20}{50} = \frac{30}{50} \times 100 = 30 \times 2 =60{/tex}

questions.question: The interest on {tex}₹{/tex} 5000 at the rate of 15% per annum for one month is
questions.explanation:
P = Rs 5000, R = 15 %, T = 1 month = {tex}\frac{1}{12} \text { years }{/tex}
Therefore, Interest = 5000 x {tex}\frac{15}{100}{/tex} x {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex} = 62.50

questions.question: Gayatri’s income is  ₹1,60,000 per year. She pays 15% of this as house rent and 10% of the remainder on her child’s education. The money left with her is
questions.explanation:
Given, Gayatri's income is  1,60,000 PA 
Amount paid towards rent = 15% of 1,60,000 = {tex}\frac{15 \times 160000}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2400000}{100}{/tex} = 24000
And 10% of the remaining is for child's education
= 160000 - 24000 = 136000 is the amount left after paying house rent
{tex}\therefore 10 \% \text { of } 136000=\frac{10}{100} \times 136000{/tex}
= 13600
= 136000 - 13600 = 122400 is the amount left after paying rent and child education.

questions.question: The ratio of Fatima’s income to her savings is 4 : 1. The percentage of money saved by her is :
questions.explanation:
Given, the ratio of income to savings of Fatima = 4 : 1
Let the income and saving be 4x and x respectively.
Percentage of money saved by her {tex}=\frac{\text { savings }}{\text { income }} \times 100 \%{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{x}{4 x} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{x}{4 x} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times 100{/tex}
= 25%

questions.question: 0.07 is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given, 0.07 
{tex}\therefore 0.07=\frac{7}{100}{/tex}
The percentage form of  {tex}\frac{7}{100}{/tex} can be written as {tex}\frac{7}{100} \times 100 \%{/tex}
= 7%

questions.question: In a scout camp, 40% of the scouts were from Gujarat State and 20% of these were from Ahmedabad. The percentage of scouts in the camp from Ahmedabad is:
questions.explanation:
Let us consider the scouts in camp = 100
Then, scouts from Gujarat = 40% of  100 = {tex}\frac{40}{100} \times 100{/tex}
= 40
Scouts from Ahmedabad = 20 % of 40 = {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 40{/tex}
= 8
Percentage of scouts from Ahmedabad = {tex}\frac{\text { Ahmedabad Scouts }}{\text { Total Scouts }} \times 100 \%{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{100} \times 100 \%{/tex}
= 8%

questions.question: What percent of  ₹4500 is  ₹9000?
questions.explanation:
Let the required percentage be x% 
{tex}\Rightarrow x \% \text { of } 4500=9000{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{x}}{100} \times 4500=9000{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 45 x=9000{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{9000}{45}{/tex}
= 200

questions.question: 5.2 is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given, 5.2
We need to find out in percentage 
{tex}5 .2=\frac{52}{10}{/tex}
The percentage form of {tex}\frac{52}{10} \text { can be written as } \frac{52}{10} \times 100 \%{/tex}
= 520%

questions.question: The ratio 3 : 8 is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here,
{tex} 3: 8=\frac{3}{8}{/tex}
The percentage form of  {tex}\frac{3}{8} \text { can be written as } \frac{3}{8} \times 100 \%{/tex}
= 37.5%

questions.question: 225% is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here,
225%
= {tex}225 \times \frac{1}{100}{/tex}  [ % is removed dividing the given number by 100 ]
{tex}=\frac{225}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{9}{4}{/tex}
Hence, the required ratio is, 9 : 4

questions.question: A bicycle is purchased for ₹1800 and is sold at a profit of 12%. Its selling price is
questions.explanation:
The cost price of a bicycle is 1800 and the bicycle is sold at 12% profit.
We know that profit {tex}=\frac{\text { Profit }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 12=\frac{\text { Profit }}{1800} \times 100{/tex}
Profit = {tex}12 \times 18{/tex} = 216
Therefore, selling price = 1800 + 216 = 2016

questions.question: A cricket bat was purchased for ₹800 and was sold for ₹1600. Then profit earned is
questions.explanation:
Given, the cost price of cricket bat = 800 and 
The selling price of cricket bat = 1600
We know that, Profit = SP - CP 
= 1600 - 800
= 800
We know that , Profit % = {tex}\frac{\text { Profit }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100 \%{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{800}{800} \times 100 \%{/tex}
= 100 %

questions.question: A farmer bought a buffalo for ₹44000 and a cow for ₹18000. He sold the buffalo at a loss of 5% but made a profit of 10% on the cow. The net result of the transaction is
questions.explanation:
Given, cost price of buffalo is 44000 and cost price of cow is 18000
Buffalo sold at loss% = 5%
And profit % for cow is 10% 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} We know that Loss% {tex}=\frac{\text { Loss }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 5=\frac{\text { Loss }}{44000} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Loss {tex}=5 \times 440{/tex} = 2200
Therefore, Selling price of cow = 44000 - 2200 = 41,800‬
Also, Profit % on the cow is 10% 
{tex}\Rightarrow \text { Profit } \%=\frac{\text { Profit }}{\mathrm{cp}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 10=\frac{\text { Profit }}{18000} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Profit = 1800
We know that SP = CP + Pofit = 18000 + 1800 = 19800
Total cost of both the animals is 44000 + 18000 = 62000
Total selling price of both the animals is 41800 + 19800 = 61600
Therefore, Net loss = CP - SP = 62000 - 61600 = 400

subtopics.name: Use of Percentages - Ratios to Percent (VII-7.2)

questions.question: If Mohan’s income is 25% more than Raman’s income, then Raman’s income is less than Mohan’s income by
questions.explanation:
Given, the income of Mohan is 25% more than the income of Raman 
Let, Raman's income be x
Thus, Mohan's income = {tex}x+25 \% \text { of } x{/tex}
{tex}=x+\frac{25}{100} x{/tex}
{tex}=x\left(1+\frac{25}{100}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{125}{100} x{/tex}
The difference between both the income is {tex}\frac{125}{100} x-x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25}{100} x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\frac{25 x}{100}}{\frac{125 x}{100}} \times 100 \%{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{25}{125} \times 100 \%=20 \%{/tex}

questions.question: The interest on ₹ 30000 for 3 years at the rate of 15% per annum is
questions.explanation:
Given, principal = 30000
Time = 3 year and Rate = 15 % 
Interest = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P} \times \mathrm{R} \times \mathrm{T}}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{30000 \times 15 \times 3}{100}{/tex}
= 13500

questions.question: Amount received on ₹ 3000 for 2 years at the rate of 11% per annum is
questions.explanation:
Given, Principal = 3000
Time = 2 years and Rate = 11%
Interest = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times T}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3000 \times 11 \times 2}{100}{/tex}
= 660 
Amount = Principal + interest = 660 + 3000
= 3660

questions.question: Interest on ₹12000 for 1 month at the rate of 10 % per annum is
questions.explanation:
Given, Principal (P) = 12000
Rate ( R) = 10% 
Time(T) = 1 month = {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex} year
We know that interest = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P} \times \mathrm{R} \times \mathrm{T}}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{12000 \times 10 \times 1}{100 \times 12}{/tex}
= 100

questions.question: Rajni and Mohini deposited ₹3000 and ₹4000 in a company at the rate of 10% per annum for 3 years and  {tex}2 \frac{1}{2}{/tex} years respectively. The difference of the amounts received by them will be
questions.explanation:
Given, For Rajini
Principal = 3000
Rate = 10%
Time = 3 years
Also, interest = {tex}=\frac{\mathrm{P} \times \mathrm{R} \times \mathrm{T}}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3000 \times 10 \times 3}{100}{/tex}
= 900
Thus, amount (A) = P + I = 3000 + 900 = 3900
And for Mohini,
Principal = 4000
Rate = 10 %
Time {tex}=2 \frac{1}{2} \text { years }=\frac{5}{2} \text { years }{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{4000 \times 10 \times 5}{100 \times 2}{/tex}
= 1000
Therefore, A = 4000 + 1000 = 5000
Hence, Difference of amount = 5000 - 3900 = 1100

questions.question: On selling an article for ₹ 329, a dealer lost 6%. The cost price of the article is
questions.explanation:
Given, selling price = 329 and loss % = 6 % 
We know that loss% = {tex}\frac{\text { Loss }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 6=\frac{\mathrm{CP}-\mathrm{SP}}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow C P - \frac{6}{100} C P=329{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{94}{100} \mathrm{CP}=329{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} CP = 350

questions.question: {tex}\frac{25 \% \text { of } 50 \% \text { of } 100 \%}{25 \times 50}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\frac{25 \% \text { of } 50 \% \text { of } 100 \%}{25 \times 50}=\frac{25}{100} \times \frac{50}{100} \times \frac{100}{100} \times\left(\frac{1}{25 \times 50}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times \frac{1}{1250}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{10000}{/tex}
= 0.01%

questions.question: The sum which will earn a simple interest of  ₹126 in 2 years at 14% per annum is
questions.explanation:
Here, Interest (I) = 126
Rate = 14% 
Time = 2 year
We know that interest = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P} \times \mathrm{R} \times \mathrm{T}}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 126=\frac{\mathrm{P} \times 14 \times 2}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 12600=\mathrm{P} \times 14 \times 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{P}=\frac{12600}{14 \times 2}{/tex} = 450

questions.question: If 11% of a number exceeds 7% of the same by 18, the number is
questions.explanation:
Let x be the given number.
Then, according to the question,
11% of x - 7% of x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4% of x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{18 \times 100}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 450

questions.question: A man spent 20% of his monthly earning on house rent. Out of the balance, he spent 75% on the other house expenses. If he had a balance of ₹250 at the end of the month, the monthly earning of the man is
questions.explanation:
Let total income be ₹x.
Then, 25% of (80% of x) = 250
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{25}{100} \times \frac{80}{100} \times x{/tex} = 250
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 250 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = ₹1250

questions.question: A boy who asked to find {tex}3 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}% of a sum of money misread the question and found {tex}5 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}% of it. His answer was ₹220, what would have been the correct answer?
questions.explanation: Since, {tex}5 \frac{1}{2} \% \equiv{/tex} 220 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}3 \frac{1}{2} \% \equiv{/tex} {tex}\frac{220 \times(\frac{7}{2})}{11 / 2}{/tex} = ₹140

questions.question: A's salary increased by 12% over last year and has become ₹ 6720. If it increased by 20% over last year's salary, then the next salary will be
questions.explanation:
Last year's salary = {tex}\frac{6720 \times 100}{100+12}{/tex} = ₹6000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The next year's salary = {tex}\frac{6000 \times 120}{100}{/tex} = ₹7200

questions.question: The ratio of the number of boys and girls in a school is 3 : 2. If 20% of the boys and 30% of the girls are scholarship holders, the percentage of the students who are not scholarship holder is
questions.explanation:
Let the number of boys and girls be 3x and 2x.
Number of those who are not scholarship holder
= (80% of 3x + 70% of 2x)
{tex}=\frac{12 x}{5}+\frac{7 x}{5}=\frac{19 x}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required percentage = {tex}\left(\frac{19 x}{5} \times \frac{1}{5 x} \times 100\right){/tex}%
= 76%

questions.question: If 15% of 40 is greater than 25% of a number by 2, then the number is
questions.explanation:
According to the given condition,
{tex}\frac{15}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 40 - {tex}\frac{25}{100}{/tex}x = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 16

questions.question: If 35% of a number is 12 less than 50% of that number, then the number is
questions.explanation:
Since, (50 - 35)% of x = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{12 \times 100}{15}{/tex} = 80

questions.question: 60% of that the students in a school are boys. If the number of girls in the school is 300, then the number of boys is
questions.explanation:
Here, 40% are girls in a school.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 40% = 300
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 60% = {tex}\frac{300 \times 60}{40}{/tex} = 450

questions.question: A sweet seller declares that he sells sweets at the cost price. However, he uses a weight of 450 g instead of 500 g. His percentage profit is
questions.explanation:
Let CP of each gram be ₹1.
Then, CP of 450 g = ₹450, SP of 450 g = ₹500
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Profit percent = {tex}\left(\frac{50}{450} \times 100\right){/tex}% = {tex}11 \frac{1}{9}{/tex}%

subtopics.name: Use of Percentages - Increase or Decrease as Percent (VII-7.2)

questions.question: A radio is sold at a gain of 16%. If it had been sold for ₹20 more, 20% would have been gained. The cost price of the radio is
questions.explanation:
Let CP of radio be ₹ x. Then,
120% of x - 116% of x = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4% of x = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{20 \times 100}{4}{/tex} = ₹500

questions.question: A dealer sold a radio at a loss of 2.5%. Had he sold it for ₹100 more, he would have gained {tex}7 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}%. In order to gain {tex}12 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}%, he should sell it for
questions.explanation:
Let CP of radio be ₹ x. Then,
{tex}107 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}% of x - {tex}97 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}% of x = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10% of x = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{100 \times 100}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ₹1000

questions.question: By selling sugar at ₹11.16 per kg, a man loses 7%. To gain 7%, it must be sold (per kg) at
questions.explanation:
Let CP of sugar per kg be ₹ x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 93% of x = 11.16
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{11.16 \times 100}{93}{/tex} = 12
So, CP = ₹12 per kg
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SP = 107% of ₹12
{tex}=\frac{107}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹12.84

questions.question: A reduction of 20% in the price of oranges enables a man to buy 5 oranges more for ₹10. The price of an orange before reduction was
questions.explanation:
Reduced price = {tex}\frac{10 \times 20}{5 \times 100}{/tex} = ₹{tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} per orange
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Original price = {tex}\frac{2 \times 100}{5 \times 80}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 50 paise

questions.question: The cost price of 19 chairs is equal to the selling price of 16 chairs. Then the gain is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Gain % = {tex}\frac{19-16}{16}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100
{tex}=\frac{3 \times 100}{16}=\frac{75}{4}{/tex} = {tex}18 \frac{3}{4}{/tex}%

questions.question: The ratio between the sales price and the cost price of an article is 7 : 5. What is the ratio between the profit and the cost price of that article?
questions.explanation:
Let SP = 7x and CP = 5x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Profit = 7x - 5x = 2x
{tex}\therefore \frac{\text { Profit }}{\mathrm{CP}}=\frac{2 x}{5 x}=\frac{2}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: By selling a table for ₹350 instead of ₹400, loss percent increased by 5%. The cost price of the table is
questions.explanation:
Given, 5% = ₹50
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100% = {tex}\frac{50}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = ₹1000

questions.question: Find interest and amount to be paid on ₹15000 at 5% per annum after 2 yr.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} Interest = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times T}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{15000 \times 5 \times 2}{100}{/tex} = ₹1500
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Amount = Principal + Interest
= ₹(15000 + 1500) = ₹16500

questions.question: A sum of money at simple interest amount to be ₹1260 in 2 yr and ₹1350 in 5 yr, then the rate per cent per annum is
questions.explanation:
Interest in three years = 1350 - 1260 = ₹90
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Interest in one year = ₹{tex}\frac{90}{3}{/tex} = ₹30
and interest for two years = ₹60
and Principal = ₹(1260 - 60)
= ₹1200
Hence, rate of interest = {tex}\frac{100 \times 60}{1200 \times 2}{/tex} = 2.5%

questions.question: The difference of 13% per annum and 12% of a sum in one year is ₹110. Then, the sum is
questions.explanation:
Let sum = ₹ x
Then, according to given condition
{tex}\frac{x \times 13 \times 1}{100}-\frac{x \times 12 \times 1}{100}{/tex} = 110
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x}{100}{/tex} = 110
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ₹11000

questions.question: Two equal amounts of money are deposited in two banks, each at 15% per annum, for {tex}3 \frac{1}{2}{/tex} yr and 5 yr. If the difference between their interest is ₹144, each sum is
questions.explanation:
Let the sum be ₹ x. Then,
{tex}\frac{x \times 5 \times 15}{100}-\frac{x \times 15}{100} \times \frac{7}{2}{/tex} = 144
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x}{100}\left(75-\frac{105}{2}\right){/tex} = 144
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{144 \times 100 \times 2}{45}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ₹640

questions.question: If the simple interest on ₹1500 increases by ₹30, when the time increases by 8 yr. The rate percent per annum is
questions.explanation:
t = 8 yr
SI increase by ₹30 in 8 yr
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SI increases in 1 yr = {tex}\frac{30}{8}{/tex} = 3.75
Let the rate be x% per annum
3.75 = {tex}\frac{1500 \times 1 \times x}{100}{/tex}
x = 0.25%

questions.question: What annual payment will discharge a debt of ₹19350 due 4 yr hence at the rate of 5% simple interest?
questions.explanation:
Let the annual instalment be ₹ x, then 4x + {interest on x for (3 + 2 + 1) yr} = 19350
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x + {tex}\frac{x \times 6 \times 5}{100}{/tex} = 19350
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = ₹4500

questions.question: A certain sum amounts to ₹7350 in 2 yr and to ₹8575 in 3 yr. The the rate percent and sum are
questions.explanation:
SI on ₹7350 for 1 yr
= ₹(8575 - 7350) = ₹1225
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Rate = {tex}\left(\frac{100 \times 1225}{7350 \times 1}\right) \%=16 \frac{2}{3} \%{/tex}
Let the sum be ₹ x.
Then, x{tex}\left(1+\frac{50}{3 \times 100}\right)^2{/tex} = 7350
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\times \frac{7}{6} \times \frac{7}{6}{/tex} = 7350
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7350 {tex}\times \frac{36}{49}{/tex} = 5400
or sum = ₹5400

subtopics.name: Prices Related to Item/Buying & Selling - Profit/Loss as a Percent (VII 7.2)

questions.question: In any organisation, 40% employees are metric pass, remaining 50% are graduates and rest 180 are post graduates. How much is the number of graduate employees?
questions.explanation:
Matric passed employees = 40%
Graduate pass = {tex}60 \times \frac{50}{100}{/tex}
= 30%
Remaining = 100 - 40 - 30
= 100 - 70
= 30%
30% = 180
100% = {tex}\frac{190}{30} \times 100{/tex}
= 600
No. of graduate passed employees = {tex}\frac{600 \times 30}{100}{/tex} = 180

questions.question: In an annual exam, Shubham got 510 marks out of 725. How much approximate percentage did he get in annual exam?
questions.explanation:
Total marks = 725
Marks obtained by Shubham = 510
Percentage required = {tex}\frac{510}{725} \times 100{/tex}
= 70.34% = 70%

questions.question: By selling 44 products, a shopkeeper get earning equals to 11 products selling price. His percentage profit is-
questions.explanation:
Missing profit % = {tex}\frac{11}{44-11} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{11}{33} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{100}{3}=33 \frac{1}{3}{/tex}%

questions.question: A fruit seller has purchased oranges at the rate of 7 in ₹10, and sold them on 40% profit. How many oranges did a customer get in ₹10?
questions.explanation:
The C.P. of 7 Oranges = ₹ 10
C.P. of 1 Orange = ₹{tex}\frac{10}{7}{/tex}
S.P. of an Orange with 40% profit = {tex}\frac{10}{7} \times \frac{140}{100}{/tex}
= ₹2
A customer gets 1 Orange for ₹2
No. of Oranges for ₹10 = {tex}\frac{10}{2}{/tex} = 5 oranges

questions.question: A money lender lends 12000 to a person on this condition that he will withdraw 10% more amount from the original after a month. Find that how much the person will return to money lender after a month?
questions.explanation:
10% of 12000 = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 12000{/tex}
= 1200
Amount to be returned after 1 month = 12000 + 1200
= ₹13200

questions.question: 63% of a number is 2583. What will be the 45% of the number?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be x
63% of number = 2583
{tex}\frac{63}{100} \times x =2583{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{2583 \times 100}{63}{/tex}
= 41 {tex}\times{/tex} 100
= 4100
45% of number = {tex}\frac{45}{100} \times 4100{/tex}
= 1845

questions.question: The ratio of Ram and Shyam’s income is 7 : 17, and the ratio of Shyam and Sohan’s income is 7 : 17, if the income of Ram is ₹490, then what is the income of Sohan?
questions.explanation:
Ram’s salary : Shyam’s salary = 7 : 17
Ram : Shyam : Sohan = 49 : 119 : 289
Let the income of Ram, Shyam and Sohan are 49x, 119x and 289x respectively.
Ram income = ₹490
4x = 490
x = {tex}\frac{490}{49}{/tex}
x = 10
Sohan's income = 289 x
= 289 {tex}\times{/tex} 10
x = ₹2890

questions.question: A company worker gets ₹440 as a salary, which is 10% more than his last year salary. What was his salary last year?
questions.explanation:
Let the last year annual salary of an employee be ₹ x
x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{110}{100}{/tex} = 440
x = {tex}\frac{440}{110}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100
x = ₹ 400

questions.question: An amount is invested for two years at a rate. If the amount had been invested at 3% more interest, then ₹ 450 are more to be got. The invested amount was:
questions.explanation:
Let the invested amount be ₹ x
{tex}\frac{x(r+3) \times 2}{100}-\frac{x \times r \times 2}{100}{/tex} = 450
2xr + bx - 2xr = 450 {tex}\times{/tex} 100
6x = 450 {tex}\times{/tex} 100
x = {tex}\frac{450 \times 100}{6}{/tex}
x = ₹ 7500

questions.question: In 80 litre mixture of milk and water, 15% is water. How much more water is to be mixed, so that the percentage of water will become 20%?
questions.explanation:
Water quantity in 80 L milk
= 15% of 80
= {tex}\frac{15}{100} \times 80{/tex}
= 12 L
Additional quantity of water to make 20% water in the mixture = x L
Total mixture = (80 + x) L
(80 + x) {tex}\times \frac{20}{100}{/tex} = 12 + x
80 + x = 60 + 5x
5x - x = 80 - 60
4x = 20
x = 5

questions.question: By selling a fan for ₹ 472, the profit is gained as {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex} of cost price. What was the percentage profit on the fan?
questions.explanation:
Let C.P. of a fan be x.
Profit % after getting {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}th of C.P. = {tex}\frac{x}{12} \times \frac{100}{x}{/tex}
= {tex}8 \frac{1}{3}{/tex}%

questions.question: If the salary of Kamal is 50% more than that of Amal, then the salary of Amal is how much less than that of Kamal?
questions.explanation:
Required percentage = {tex}\frac{50}{150} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}33 \frac{1}{3} \%{/tex}

questions.question: There is a ratio of 10 : 3 in between selling price and profit. What was the profit percentage on selling of the product?
questions.explanation:
Profit percent = {tex}\frac{3 \times 100}{10-3}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{300}{7}=42 \frac{6}{7}{/tex}%

questions.question: A person gets a loss of {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} of the cost price by selling a shirt at ₹ 600. At what price would he sell the shirt so that he can make a profit of {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} of cost price?
questions.explanation:
Let the C.P. of a shirt be ₹ 100
S.P. = ({tex}\frac{100}{120}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100)
{tex}=\frac{500}{6}{/tex}
S.P. = {tex}\frac{500}{6}{/tex}
C.P. = ₹ 100
{tex}\frac{500}{6} \rightarrow{/tex} ₹ 100
{tex}1 \rightarrow \frac{100 \times 6}{500}{/tex}
600 {tex}\to{/tex} ₹ {tex}\frac{100 \times 6 \times 600}{500}{/tex}
₹ 720
New S.P. at {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} profit of C.P.
= 720 + 720 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}
= 720 + 120
= ₹ 840

questions.question: The price of 10 chairs is equal to the price of 4 tables. The total price of 15 chairs and 2 tables is ₹4000. The total price of 12 chairs and 3 tables is-
questions.explanation:
10 chairs = 4 tables
15 chairs = {tex}\frac{4}{10} \times 15{/tex} tables
= 6 tables
cost of (6 + 2) tables = ₹4000
12 chairs + 3 tables = {tex}12 \times \frac{4}{10}+3{/tex}
= 4.8 + 3
= 7.8 tables
Price of 7.8 tables = {tex}\frac{4000}{8} \times 7.8{/tex}
= ₹3900

questions.question: The ratio of cost price and selling price is 5 : 4, percentage loss is-
questions.explanation:
Ratio = 5 - 4
C.P. = ₹100
S.P. = ₹80
Loss = ₹20
Loss % = {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 100{/tex} = 20%

questions.question: If the price of {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} part of an asset is ₹16800, then what will be the price of its {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} part?
questions.explanation:
Price of {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} th part = 16800
Price of 1 part = {tex}16800 \times \frac{5}{4}{/tex}
Price of {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} part = {tex}16800 \times \frac{5}{4} \times \frac{3}{9}{/tex} = ₹9000

questions.question: Find out the percentage of profit by selling a product at a certain price, if it is sold on {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} of its price, then the loss can be of 10%-
questions.explanation:
Let the S.P be ₹100
90% = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}th of S.P. = {tex}\frac{3}{4} \times 100{/tex}
= ₹75
100% = {tex}\frac{75}{90} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{₹ 250}{3}{/tex}
Profit = {tex}100-\frac{250}{3}{/tex}
= ₹{tex}\frac{50}{3}{/tex}
Profit % = {tex}\frac{\frac{50}{250}}{\frac{3}{3}} \times 100{/tex} = 20%

questions.question: If purchase price is ₹2516 and selling price is ₹2272, then the percentage loss is
questions.explanation:
Loss % = {tex}\frac{(2516-2272)}{2516} \times 100{/tex}%
= {tex}\frac{244 \times 100}{2516}{/tex}
= 9.69%

questions.question: A horse and a cow each sold at ₹12000, horse was sold on 20% loss and cow was sold on 20% profit. The result of entire transaction is-
questions.explanation:
Let the C.P. of the horse be ₹x
S.P. at 20% loss = {tex}\frac{80}{100} x{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4 x}{5}{/tex}
A/q
{tex}\frac{4 x}{5}{/tex} = ₹12000
x = {tex}\frac{₹ 12000 \times 5}{4}{/tex}
= ₹15000
Let the C.P. of the cow be ₹y.
S.P. at 20% profit = {tex}\frac{120}{100} y=\frac{6}{5} y{/tex}
A/q
{tex}\frac{6}{5} y{/tex} = 12000
y = {tex}\frac{12000 \times 5}{6}{/tex}
y = 10000
Total C.P. = 15000 + 10000
= ₹25000
Total S.P. = 12000 + 12000
= ₹24000
Loss = ₹(25000 - 24000)
= ₹1000

questions.question: In how many years, simple interest will be {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} of principal amount at 8% annual interest?
questions.explanation:
Let the principal be ₹P
Time = {tex}\frac{S . I \times 100}{P \times R}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac{2}{5} P \times 100}{P \times 8}{/tex}
= 5 years

questions.question: Suhash has beared a loss of 25% by selling a product for ₹ 7500. To gain a profit 25% at what price he might have sold the product?
questions.explanation:
A/q
S.P. = x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{75}{100}{/tex}
x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{75}{100}{/tex} = 7500
x = 10000
S.P. for 25% profit
= 10000 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{125}{100}{/tex}
= ₹ 12500

questions.question: 33% marks are required to pass an examination. A student scored 158 marks and failed by 7 marks. The maximum marks in the examination are
questions.explanation:
Let maximum marks be x.
According to the question, {tex}x \times \frac{33}{100}{/tex} = 158 + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{165 \times 100}{33}{/tex} = 500

questions.question: A TV with marked price ₹16000 is available on 15% discount. The selling price of TV is
questions.explanation:
SP = {tex}MP \times \frac{100-\text { discount }}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}16000 \times \frac{100-15}{100}{/tex}
= 160 {tex}\times{/tex} 85 = ₹13600

questions.question: An amount becomes ₹2240 in 2 years and ₹2600 in 5 years at simple interest. Then the amount is
questions.explanation:
Principal + 2 year interest = ₹2440 ...(i)
Principal + 5 year interest = ₹2600 ...(ii)
From equation (ii) subtract equation (i)
3 year interest = ₹360
2 year interest = {tex}\frac{360}{3} \times 2{/tex}
= ₹240
Principal = 2240 - 240 = ₹2000

questions.question: If the x% of 350 is 21, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation:
x = {tex}\frac{350 \times x}{100}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{210}{55}{/tex}
x = 6

questions.question: The difference between 55% of a number and its 14% is 8610. What will be the 85% of the number?
questions.explanation:
Difference required = (55 - 14%)
= 41%
A/q
41% {tex}\to{/tex} 8610
85% = {tex}\frac{8610}{41}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 85
= 210 {tex}\times{/tex} 85
= ₹ 17850

questions.question: Navin purchased a cellphone for ₹12000 and sold it for ₹8000, then his loss per cent, is
questions.explanation:
Given, CP = ₹12000, SP = ₹8000
Loss = CP - SP = 12000 - 8000 = ₹4000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Loss % = {tex}\frac{\text { Loss }}{\text { Cost price }} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4000}{12000} \times 100=\frac{100}{3}{/tex}% = {tex}33 \frac{1}{3}{/tex}%

questions.question: What will be the average selling price of five products of ₹27, ₹30, ₹26, ₹29 & ₹38, if sold on 20% profit?
questions.explanation:
Total price of 5 items = 27 + 30 + 26 + 29 + 38
= ₹150
Profit of 20% on S.P. = {tex}\frac{150 \times 120}{100}{/tex}
= ₹180
Average S.P. of 1 item = {tex}\frac{180}{5}{/tex}
= ₹36

questions.question: If the cost price of 25 pens is ₹56.25, then at what rate per pen is to be sold so that the profit will become 16%?
questions.explanation:
C.P. of 25 pens = ₹56.25
S.P. on 16% profit = {tex}56.25 \times \frac{116}{100}{/tex}
= ₹65.25
S.P. of one pen = {tex}\frac{65.25}{25}{/tex}
= ₹2.61

questions.question: If lemons were bought at ₹48 per dozen and sold at the rate of ₹48 per 10, then the gain or loss percent, is
questions.explanation: 20% profit

questions.question: Sameer receives ₹ 65,000 as commission on selling a car, which is 5% of the selling price. At what price does Sameer sell the car?
questions.explanation:
Let the selling price of the car be ₹ x
Commission = 5% of selling price = {tex}₹\left(\frac{5}{100} x\right){/tex}
Since, Sameer receives ₹ 65000 as commission
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{5}{100} x{/tex} = 65000 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}65000 \times \frac{100}{5}{/tex} = 1300000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The selling price of the car is ₹ 1300000

questions.question: A book was sold for ₹ 27.50 with a profit of 10%. If it was sold for ₹ 25.75, then what would have been the percentage of profit or loss?
questions.explanation:
Selling price of book = ₹ 27.50
Profit % = 10%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C.P. = {tex}₹ \frac{100}{110} \times 27.50{/tex} = ₹ 25
Now, new selling price = ₹ 25.75
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Profit = ₹ (25.75 - 25) = ₹ 0.75
Now, Profit % = {tex}\frac{0.75}{25} \times 100{/tex} = 3%

questions.question: Find the interest on ₹ 14500 at 18% per annum for a period of 3 years and 4 months.
A sum becomes ₹ 6050 after two years and ₹ 7320.50 after four years. The sum is ________.

The simple interest on a certain sum for 3 years and 9 months at 15 % per annum is ₹ 1200 less than the simple interest on the same sum for 4 years and 8 months at 12 % per annum. Find the sum (in ₹).
A shopkeeper gains 25% while buying an article and 32% while selling them. His total gain is ________.
If M is increased by 30%, it equals N. If N is decreased by 40%, it equals P. Then P is how many% of M.
A man purchases goods wroth ₹ 9000 and sold half of them at a gain of 15%. at what gain percent must he sell the remainder to get a gain of 25% on whole?
If the selling price is doubled, the profit quadruples. Find the profit percent.
The simple interest at x % for x years will be ₹ x on a sum of ________.
questions.explanation:
S.l. = ₹ x, Rate = x%, Time = x years,
S.I. = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times T}{100}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{P \times x \times x}{100}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}₹\left(\frac{100}{x}\right){/tex}

questions.question: Simple interest on a certain amount is {tex}\frac{9}{16}{/tex} of the principal. If the numbers representing the rate of interest (in percent) and time (in years) be equal, then time for which the principal is lent out, is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the principal be ₹ P.
And the rate of interest (in percent) = Time (in years) = n (say)
According to question,
S.I. = {tex}\frac{9}{16} P{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{P \times n \times n}{100}=\frac{9}{16} P{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n2 = {tex}\frac{9}{16} P \times \frac{100}{P}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = {tex}\frac{15}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} n = {tex}7 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: If 15% of an amount is ₹ 690 then find ₹ 66% of the amount.
A farmer bought a buffalo for ₹ 64000 and a cow for ₹ 27000. He sold the buffalo at a profit of 8% but made a loss of 18% on the cow. The net result of the transactions is ________.
questions.explanation: 0.29 % profit

questions.question: The marks obtained by three students in a test are in the ratio of 2 : 3 : 4. If maximum marks obtained among them is 36 then it is how much percent of the sum of the marks obtained by them?
questions.explanation: {tex}44\frac 49 \%{/tex}

questions.question:
Two numbers are in the ratio 3 : 5. If 9 is subtracted from each, then the ratio becomes 12 : 23. The smaller number
is ________.
questions.explanation:
Let the numbers be 3x and 5x.
According to question, 
{tex}\frac{3 x-9}{5 x-9}=\frac{12}{23} \Rightarrow{/tex} 23(3x - 9) = 12(5x - 9)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 69x - 207 = 60x - 108 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = 99 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 11
So, the numbers are 33 and 55
Hence, smallest number is 33

questions.question: What percent of 1 litre is 50 cm3?
questions.explanation:
1L = 1000 cm3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required percentage = {tex}\frac {50}{1000} \times 100{/tex} = 5%

questions.question: How many kg of Basmati rice costing ₹ 45/kg should a shopkeeper mix with 18 kg of ordinary rice costing ₹ 22/kg so that he can get a profit 20% of on selling the mixture at ₹ 42/kg.
150 % of a number is 900. What is the number?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be x.
According to question, 150% of x = 900
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac {150}{100}\times{/tex} x 900 {tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac {900 \times 100}{150}{/tex} = 600

questions.question: Which of the following is greater than 16.3%?
questions.explanation: 113 out of 250 = {tex}\frac {113}{250} \times{/tex} 100 = 45.2%

questions.question: In an election out of 60,000 eligible voters 38000 cast their votes. What was the percentage of people not casting their votes?

The ratio of the length of rope A to the length of rope B is 3 : 4. The ratio of the length of rope C to the length of rope B is 7 : 6. If the length of the longest rope is 84 cm, then find the total length of the three ropes.
questions.explanation:
Let the length of rope A be 3x and length of rope B be 4 x.
Given that, {tex}\frac{\text { Length of rope } C}{\text { Length of rope } B}=\frac{7}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Length of rope C = {tex}\frac{7}{6} \times{/tex} Length of rope B
= {tex}\frac{7}{6} \times 4 x=\frac{14 x}{3}{/tex}
As rope C is the longest rope.
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac {14x}3 = 84 \Rightarrow{/tex} x = 18
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of rope A = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 54cm
Length of rope B = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 72 cm
Hence, the total length of three ropes
= 54 + 72 + 84 = 210 cm

questions.question: A man spends 88 % of his annual income. If he saves ₹ 4800 every month, find his annual income.
{tex}\frac{25 \% \text { of } 50 \% \text { of } 100 \%}{25 \text { of } 100 \times 50 \% \text { of } 100}{/tex} is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, {tex}\frac{25 \% \text { of } 50 \% \text { of } 100 \%}{25 \text { of } 100 \times 50 \% \text { of } 100}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{25}{100} \times \frac{50}{100} \times \frac{100}{100}\right) \div{/tex}{tex}\left(25 \times 100 \times \frac{50}{100} \times 100\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times \frac{1}{25 \times 100 \times 50}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{10000} \times \frac{1}{100}{/tex}
= 0.0001 %

questions.question: What percent of a day is 18 minutes?
questions.explanation:
1 day = 24 hrs
1 hr = 60 mins
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 day = (60 {tex}\times{/tex} 24) mins = 1440 mins
Required percentage = {tex}\frac {18}{1440} \times{/tex} 100 = 1.25%

questions.question: 11 oranges are bought for ₹ 10 and 10 oranges are sold for ₹ 11. The gain/loss percent is ________.
questions.explanation:
C.P. of 11 oranges = ₹ 10
{tex}\therefore {/tex} C.P. of 1 orange = ₹{tex}\left(\frac{10}{11}\right){/tex}
Also, S.P. of 10 oranges = ₹ 11
{tex}\therefore {/tex} S.P. of 1 orange = ₹{tex}\left(\frac{11}{10}\right){/tex}
Now, Profit = S.P. - C.P. = {tex}\frac{11}{10}-\frac{10}{11}=₹\left(\frac{21}{110}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \text {Profit} \%=\frac{\frac{21}{110}}{\frac{10}{11}} \times 100{/tex} = 21 %

questions.question: The simple interest on a sum of money is {tex}\frac 49{/tex} of the principal. Find the rate percent and time, if both are numerically equal.
questions.explanation:
Let principal be ₹ x, rate be n % and time be n years.
According to the question,
Simple interest = {tex}\frac{4}{9} x \Rightarrow \frac{x \times n \times n}{100}=\frac{4}{9} \times x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x \times n^2}{x}=\frac{4}{9} \times 100 \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}n^2=\frac{400}{9} \Rightarrow n=\frac{20}{3}=6 \frac{2}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Time, n = 6{tex}\frac 23{/tex} years = 6 years 8 months
Rate = n % = 6{tex}\frac 23{/tex} %

questions.question: Express 54 m as a percentage of 1 km 80 m.
questions.explanation:
1 km 80 m = 1000 m + 80 m = 1080 m
Required percentage = {tex}\frac {54}{1080} \times{/tex} 100 = 5%

questions.question: In a fruit godown, 1600 kg mangoes are stored. If 23% of them are rotten, then how much mangoes are good in the godown?
questions.explanation:
Total quantity of mangoes = 1600 kg
Quantity of mangoes rotten = {tex}\frac{23 \times 1600}{100}{/tex} = 368 kg
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Quantity of good mangoes = 1600 - 368 = 1232 kg

questions.question: A man sells two horses for ₹ 4000 each, neither losing nor gaining in the deal. If he sold one horse at a gain of 25 %, then the other horse is sold at a loss of ________.
questions.explanation:
Selling price of 1 horse = ₹ 4000
Profit % = 25 %
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C.P. = {tex}\frac{100 \times \text { S.P. }}{100+\text { Profit } \%}=\frac{100 \times 4000}{125}{/tex}
= ₹ 3200
Since, the man is having neither loss nor gain.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost price of second horse
= S.P. of 2 horses - C.P. of 1 horse
= ₹ (8000 - 3200) = ₹ 4800
Now, cost price of 2nd horse = ₹ 4800
Selling price = ₹ 4000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Loss = C.P. - S.P. = ₹ 800
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Loss % = {tex}\frac{800}{4800} \times{/tex} 100 = 16{tex}\frac 23{/tex} %

questions.question: Priyanka bought 80 pears for ₹ 40. Out of them, 25 pears were spoiled and thrown away. She sold the remaining pears at a profit of 10%. Find the selling price of 1 pear.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of 80 pears = ₹ 40 Profit = 10%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling price = {tex}\frac{100+\text { Profit % }}{100} \times{/tex} C.P.
= {tex}\frac{100+10}{100} \times 40=\frac{110 \times 40}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 44
Since, 25 pears were spoiled.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Pears left = 80 - 25 = 55
S.P. of 55 pears = ₹ 44
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S.P. of 1 pear = {tex}\frac{44}{55}{/tex} = ₹ 0.80

questions.question: Kavita purchased a dozen handkerchiefs for 60 and sold them for ₹ 84 whereas Pooja purchased a dozen handkerchiefs for ₹ 90 and sold them for ₹ 150. Whose profit percent is more and by how much?
questions.explanation:
For Kavita,
C.P. of 12 handkerchiefs = ₹ 60
S.P. of 12 handkerchiefs = ₹ 84
Profit = S.P. - C.P. = ₹(84 - 60) = ₹ 24
Profit % = {tex}\frac{24}{60} \times 100{/tex} = 40 %
For Pooja,
C.P. of 12 handkerchiefs = ₹ 90
S.P. of 12 handkerchiefs = ₹ 150
Profit = S.P. - C.P = ₹(150 - 90) = ₹ 60
Profit % = {tex}\frac{60}{90} \times 100{/tex} = 66.6 %
Pooja's profit percent is more by (66.6 - 40) % = 26.6 %

questions.question: The price of a commodity is reduced to its {tex}\frac 79{/tex}th. What is the percentage reduction in the price?
questions.explanation: {tex}22.\bar 2{/tex}

questions.question: Kavita invited 24 of her friends on her birthday party. In the party, the ratio of boys and girls was 3 : 5 (excluding Kavita). Find the number of boys and girls invited by Kavita on her birthday party respectively.
questions.explanation:
Let the number of boys and girls be 3x and 5x respectively.
According to question,
3x + 5x = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of boys = 9
Number of girls = 15

questions.question: A school has 560 students. The number of girls is 14{tex}\frac 27{/tex}% of the number of boys. How many girls are there in the school?
questions.explanation:
Let the number of boys be x.
Then, the number of girls = 14{tex}\frac 27{/tex}% of x = {tex}\frac {100}7\times \frac 1{100}x = \frac x7{/tex}
Now, total number of students = 560
So, x + {tex}\frac{x}{7}{/tex} = 560 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{8 x}{7}{/tex} = 560 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 490
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number of girls = 560 - 490 = 70

questions.question: During a cricket match, 4800 people showed their spirit by wearing home team’s colour. If 12000 people attended the game, then what percent of crowd wear home team’s colour?
questions.explanation: Percentage of crowd wearing home team's colour = {tex}\frac{4800}{12000} \times 100{/tex} = 40 %

questions.question: A farmer borrowed ₹ 5500 at 8 % per annum. After 5 years, he cleared the account by giving ₹ 6000 and a cow. Find the cost of the cow.
questions.explanation:
Principal = ₹ 5500
Rate = 8 %
Time = 5 years
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S.I. = {tex}\frac{5500 \times 8 \times 5}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 2200
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Amount = S.I. + Principal
= ₹ (2200 + 5500) = ₹ 7700
According to question,
₹ 6000 + cost of cow = ₹ 7700
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cost of cow = ₹ (7700 - 6000) = ₹ 1700

questions.question: The number of seats for admission is increased by 10% every year. If the number of seats in 2001 was 400, what were the number of seats in 2003?
questions.explanation: 400 {tex}\times \frac{110}{100} \times \frac{110}{100}{/tex} = 484

questions.question: In measuring the sides of a rectangle errors of 5% and 3% in excess are made. The error percent in the calculated area is
questions.explanation:
Let actual sides of rectangle are 'l’ and ‘b’ respectively.
Actually measured sides are (l + 0.05l) and (b + 0.03b) i.e. 1.05l and 1.03b
Measured area = (1.05l) (1.03b) = 1.0815lb
Actual Area = lb
Error in area= 1.0815lb - 0.0815lb
{tex}\therefore{/tex} % error = {tex}\frac{0.0815 l b}{l b}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100% = 8.15%

questions.question: A man sold a watch for ₹ 60 and lost something. Had he sold it for ₹ 75, his gain would have been double the former loss. Find the cost price of the watch.
questions.explanation:
Let loss = ₹ x
S.P. = ₹ 60
C.P. = (60 + x)
If S.P. is ₹ 75, then gain = 2x
So, S.P. = C.P. + gain
75 = 60 + x + 2x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 5
So, C.P. = 60 + 5 = 65.

questions.question: A number is increased by 10% and then reduced by 10%, then the number
questions.explanation:
Let the number be ‘x’.
10% of x {tex}\frac{x}{10}{/tex}
Number increased by 10% i.e. number
= {tex}x+\frac{x}{10}=\frac{11 x}{10}{/tex}
Now, 10% of {tex}\frac{11 x}{10}=\frac{11 x}{100}{/tex}
Now, number {tex}\frac{11 x}{10}{/tex} reduced by 10% i.e.
number = {tex}\frac{11 x}{10}-\frac{11 x}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{110 x-11 x}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{99 x}{100}{/tex} = 900% of X
Thus, we get that the number is decreased by 1%.

questions.question: Rajni’s salary was reduced by 10%. In order to reach her salary back to the original amount it must be raised by
questions.explanation:
Let the original amount = ₹x
10% of x = x {tex}\times \frac{10}{100}=\frac{x}{10}{/tex}
Salary reduced by 10% = {tex}x-\frac{x}{10}=\frac{9 x}{10}{/tex}
To reach at original amount, let it raised by amount = S.
{tex}\therefore \frac{9 x}{10}+S{/tex} = x {tex}\Rightarrow S=\frac{x}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore S=\frac{\frac{x}{10}} {\frac{9}{10}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100 x}{9}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} amount raised w.r.t {tex}\frac{9 x}{10}{/tex})
S = {tex}11 \frac{1}{9} \%{/tex}

questions.question: A cloth merchant decides to sell his material at the cost price, but measures 80 cm for a metre. His gain % is.
questions.explanation:
Error in measurement = 100 - 80 = 20 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} % gain = {tex}\left(\frac{\text { Error }}{\text { True value-Error }} \times 100\right) \%{/tex}
% gain = {tex}\left(\frac{20}{100-20}\right) \times 100 \%{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{20 \times 100}{80} \%{/tex}
= 25%

questions.question: Rajnish purchased 120 rims of paper at ₹ 80 per rim. He spent ₹ 280 on transportation, paid octroi at the rate of 40 paise per rim and paid ₹ 72 to the coolie. If he wants to have a gain of 8%, the selling price per rim must be
questions.explanation:
Cost price to Hemant
= (120 {tex}\times{/tex} 80 + 280 + 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 120 + 72)
= ₹ 10,000
Selling price = cost price + 8% gain
= 10000 {tex}+\frac{8}{100} \times 1000{/tex}
= ₹ 10800 [for 120 Rims]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling cost / Rim = {tex}\frac{10800}{120}{/tex} = ₹ 90

questions.question: If a person makes a profit of 10% on {tex}\frac 14{/tex}th of the quantity sold and a loss of 20% on the rest, then his average percent profit or loss is
questions.explanation:
Let CP = ₹ 100
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Gain on {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}th i.e. ₹ 25 = ₹ 2.5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SP = ₹ 27.5
Loss on {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}th i.e. ₹ 75 = 20% of 75 = ₹ 15
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Selling price (SP) = 75 - 15 = ₹ 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total SP= 60 + 27.5 = 87.5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Loss = 100 - 87.5 = ₹ 12.5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} % Loss {tex}=\left(\frac{12.5}{100} \times 100\right){/tex}
% Loss = 12.5%

questions.question: A man had ₹ 1200, part of which he lent at 5% and the remaining at 4% he got ₹ 106 as interest after 2 years. The amount lent at 5% is
questions.explanation:
Let x be lent at 5% and (1200 - x) at 4%
Then we have,
{tex}\frac{x \times 5 \times 2}{100}+\frac{(1200-x) \times 4 \times 2}{100}{/tex} = 106
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 500.

questions.question: Achal bought a second-hand car for ₹ 2,25,000 and spend ₹ 25,000 for repairing. If he sold it for ₹ 3,25,000, what is his profit per cent?
questions.explanation:
C.P. of car = ₹ 2,25,000
Cost of repairing = ₹ 25,000
Actual C.P. = 2,25,000 + 25,000
= ₹ 2,50,000
S.P. of car = ₹ 3,25,000
Profit = S.P. - C.P.
= 3,25,000 - 2,50,000 = ₹ 75,000
Profit % = {tex}\frac{\text { Profit }}{\text { C.P. }} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{75000}{250000} \times{/tex} 100 = 30%

questions.question: There is a ratio of 5 : 4 between two numbers. If 40% of the first number is 12 then what would be the 50% of the second number?
questions.explanation:
Let the required numbers are 5x and 4x, he {tex}5 x \times \frac{40}{100}=12{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}x=\frac{12 \times 100}{5 \times 40}{/tex} = 6
50% of second number
= {tex}4 x \times \frac{50}{100}{/tex} = {tex}4 \times 6 \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 12

questions.question: The numerator and denominator of a fraction are in the ratio of 2 : 3. If 6 is subtracted from 54. the numerator, the result is a fraction that has a value {tex}\frac 23{/tex} of the original fraction. The numerator of the original fraction is
questions.explanation:
Let the fraction be {tex}\frac{2 x}{3 x}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{2 x-6}{3 x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2 x}{3 x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 6 = {tex}\frac{4 x}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 18 = 4x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 9
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Numerator = 2 x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 =18

questions.question: Seats for mathematics, physics and biology in a school are in the ratio 5 : 7 : 8. There is a proposal to increase these seats by 40 %, 50% and 75% respectively. The ratio of increased seats will be
questions.explanation:
Let the number of seats for mathematics, physics and biology be 5x, 7x and 8x respectively.
No of increased seats are (140% of 5x), (150% of 7x) and (175% of 8x)
i.e., {tex}\left(\frac{140}{100} \times 5 x\right),\left(\frac{150}{100} \times 7 x\right){/tex} and {tex}\left(\frac{175}{100} \times 8 x\right){/tex}
i.e. 7x, {tex}\frac{21}{2} x{/tex}, 14x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio 7x {tex}: \frac{21}{2} x:{/tex} 14x
i.e. 14x : 21x : 28x
= 2 : 3 : 4

subtopics.name: Charge given on Borrowed Money/Simple Interest - Interest for Multi Yr (VII 7.2)

questions.question: A sells a bicycle to B at a profit of 20%. B sells it to C at a profit of 25%. If C pays ₹ 225 to it, the cost price of the bicycle for A is
questions.explanation:
Let CP for A = ₹ x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} CP of B = ₹ 1.2x
and CP for C = ₹ 1.5x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1.5x = 225
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{225}{1.5}{/tex} = ₹ 150
{tex}\therefore{/tex} CP for A = ₹ 150

questions.question: Marotrao Patil sold a car for ₹ 2,75,000 that he had bought at ₹ 300000. What is his percent loss?
questions.explanation:
C.P. of car = ₹ 300,000
S. P. of car = ₹ 2,75,000
Loss = ₹ 3, 00,000 - ₹ 2,75000 = ₹ 25,000
Loss% = {tex}\frac{L}{C P} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{25000}{300000} \times 100{/tex}

questions.question: A honeybee colony that contains 50,000 bees in late fall may have only 10,000 bees at the end of winter. What percent of the bees live through the winter season?
questions.explanation:
Total number of bees = 50,000
Bees left till the end of winter = 10,000
Percentage of bees living through winter season
= {tex}\frac{10,000}{50,000} \times 100{/tex} = 20%

questions.question: Sonali invests 15% of her monthly salary in insurance policies. She spends 55% of her monthly salary on shopping and household expenses. She saves the remaining amount of ₹ 12,750. What is Sonali’s monthly income?
questions.explanation:
Let Sonali's monthly income = ₹ x
15% of x + 55% of x + 12750 = x
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{70}{100} x{/tex} + 12750 = x
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 12750 = {tex}\frac{30 \mathrm{x}}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x = {tex}\frac{12750 \times 10}{30}{/tex}= ₹ 42,500

questions.question: The CP of 25 articles is equal to the SP of 20 articles. Then gain % is
questions.explanation:
Gain %
= {tex}\frac{\text { No.of articles on CP }- \text { No. of articles on SP }}{\text { No.of articles on SP }} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{25-20}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 25%

questions.question: A person sells 36 oranges per rupee and incurs a loss of 4%. Find how many oranges per rupee to be sold to have a gain of 8%?
questions.explanation:
We have
{tex}\frac{SP_1}{100+x_1}=\frac{S P_2}{100+x_2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\frac{1}{36}}{100-4}=\frac{\mathrm{SP}_2}{100+8}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{SP}_2=\frac{1}{36} \times \frac{108}{96}=\frac{1}{32}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} He has to sell 32 oranges per rupee.

questions.question: A customer saves ₹ 15 when a discount of 20% on the marked price is offered. What did he pay for the article?
questions.explanation:
Let M.P be x
Discount = {tex}\frac{20 x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{20 x}{100}{/tex} = 15
x = 75
S.P. = M.P. - Discount = 75 - 15 = ₹ 60

questions.question: A large watermelon weighs 20 kg with 96% of its weight being water. It is allowed to study in the sun and some of the water evaporation so that now, only 95%, of its weight is water. Its reduced weight will be
questions.explanation:
96% of 20 kg = {tex}\frac{96}{100} \times 20{/tex} = 19.2 kg ... [wt. of water]
Let 'x' kg of water in evaporated, then
19.2 - x = 95% of (20 - x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 19.2 - x {tex}=\frac{95 \times(20-x)}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1920 - 100x = 1900 - 95x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 4 kg
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Reduced wt = 20 - 4 = 16 kg

questions.question: A milkman borrowed ₹ 2500 from two money lenders. For one loan, he paid 5% p.a. and for the other, he paid 7% p.a. The total interest paid after two years was ₹ 275. Calculate the money he borrowed at 7% interest?
questions.explanation:
Total SI = ₹ 275.
Let x be the sum borrowed at 7% rate.
So {tex}\frac{(2500-x) \times 5 \times 2}{100}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{x \times 7 \times 2}{100}{/tex} = 275
x = ₹ 625.

questions.question: Of a certain sum, {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}rd is invested at 3%, {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}th at 6% and the rest at 8%. If the SI for 2 years from all these investments amounts to ₹ 600. Then the original sum was
questions.explanation:
Remaining part = {tex}1-\left(\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{6}\right)=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Average rate % per annum (R)
= {tex}\left(\frac{1}{3} \times 3\right)+\left(\frac{1}{6} \times 6\right)+\left(\frac{1}{2} \times 8\right){/tex} = 6%
SI = ₹ 600
T = 2 years, P = ?
{tex}I=\frac{P T R}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{P}=\frac{100 \times 1}{\mathrm{TR}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100 \times 600}{2 \times 6}{/tex} = ₹ 5000

questions.question: Articles are bought at 42 paise a dozen and sold at 85 paise a score (A group of twenty). The profit percent is
questions.explanation:
85 paise a score would be
{tex}85 \times \frac{12}{20}{/tex} = 51 paise a dozen as selling price.
{tex}\frac{51-42}{42} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}21 \frac{3}{7} \%{/tex}

questions.question: A publisher sells books to a retail dealer at ₹ 5 a copy but allows 25 copies to count as 24. If the retailer sells each of the 25 copies at ₹ 6, his profit per cent is
questions.explanation:
The cost of 25 copies to the retailer = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = ₹ 120.
The SP of 25 copies = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = ₹ 150
Profit = {tex}\frac{150-120}{120} \times 100{/tex} = 25%

questions.question: The price of wheat has increased by 60%. In order to restore the original price, the new price must be reduced by
questions.explanation:
The % of reduction = {tex}\frac{60}{100+60} \times 100{/tex}
= 37.5%

questions.question: A dishonest dealer professes to sell his goods at cost price, but uses a weight of 900 grams for a kilogram weight. Find his real gain percent.
questions.explanation:
He is gaining a C.P. of 100 grams on every 900 grams.
So, gain{tex}\%{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{900} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}11 \frac{1}{9} \%{/tex}

questions.question: If 10% of m is the same as 20% of n then m : n is equal to:
questions.explanation:
10% of m = {tex}\mathrm{m} \times \frac{10}{100}=\frac{m}{10}{/tex}
20% of n = {tex}n \times \frac{20}{100}=\frac{n}{5}{/tex}
According to the question,
{tex}\frac{m}{10}=\frac{n}{5} \Rightarrow \frac{m}{2}=\frac{n}{1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m : n = 2: 1

questions.question: A merchant buys 200 kg of rice at ₹ 7.25 per kg, 400 kg of rice at ₹ 5.75 per kg. He mixes them and sells one third of mixture at ₹ 6 per kg. At what rate should he sell the remaining mixture so that he may earn a profit of 20% on the whole out lay?
questions.explanation:
C.P. of 200 kg of rice = (7.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 200)
= 1450
C.P. of 400 kg of rice = (5.75 {tex}\times{/tex} 400)
= 2300
Total C.P of 600 kg of mixture
= (1450 + 2300) = 3750
profit required on whole outlay = 20%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} S.P must be {tex}=\left[\frac{120}{100} \times 3750\right]{/tex} = 4500
Now, one third of the mixture
= {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 600 = 200 kg
S.P. of this 200 kg of mixture = [6 {tex}\times{/tex} 200]
= ₹ 120
S.P. of remaining 400 kg = [4500 - 1200]
= ₹ 3300
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Rate of selling the remaining
= {tex}\left[\frac{3300}{400}\right]{/tex} = 8.25 Per kg.

questions.question: A man loses {tex}12 \frac{1}{2} \%{/tex} of his money and after spending 70% of the remainder, he was left with ₹ 210. The money that he had at the beginning
questions.explanation:
Let money = x
{tex}12 \frac{1}{2} \% {/tex} of {tex}x=\frac{x}{8} {/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} loss = {tex}\frac{x}{8}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Remainder money {tex}\frac{x-x}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7 x}{8}{/tex}
70% of remainde = {tex}\frac{7 x}{8} \times \frac{70}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{49 x}{80}{/tex}
i.e. {tex}\frac{49 x}{80}{/tex} amount spends.
Now, left amount = {tex}\frac{7 x}{8}-\frac{49 x}{80}{/tex} = 210
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{70 x-49 x}{80}{/tex} = 210
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}x=\frac{210 \times 80}{21}{/tex} = 800
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Money at the beginning = ₹ 800

questions.question: A man bought goods worth ₹ 6000 and sold half of them at a gain of 10%. At what gain percent must he sell the remainder to get a gain of 25% on the whole?
questions.explanation:
Total CP = ₹6000
Profit = 25%
overall
SP = {tex}6000 \times \frac{100+25}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 7500
SP of half of the goods
= {tex}3000 \times \frac{100+10}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 3300
SP of remaining half goods
= 7500 - 3300 = ₹ 4200.
Profit on remaining half goods costing
= ₹ 3000
= ₹ 4200 - ₹ 3000 = ₹ 1200.
Profit % = {tex}\frac{1200}{3000} \times 100{/tex} = 40%

questions.question: A person invested 3 times as much money at 5% as he had invested at 2% per annum simple interest. Further, he invested ₹ 6000 more at 3% than he had invested at 2%. If the total interest from the three investments after a year is ₹ 980, then the total amount he invested is
questions.explanation:
Let he invested x amount of ₹ at 2%
then {tex}\frac{3 x \times 5}{100}+\frac{2 x}{100}+\frac{(x+6000) \times 3}{100}{/tex} = 980
20x+ 18,000 = 98,000
20x = 80,000
x = 4000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total amount he invested
= 3x + 2x + 3x + 6000 = 26000

questions.question: In an examination 52% candidates failed in English, 42% in maths and 17% in both. The percentage of those passed in both the subjects is:
questions.explanation:
Failed in Maths = 42
Failed in English = 52
Failed in both = 17
Failed in English only = 52 - 17 = 35
Failed in Maths only = 42 - 17 = 25
Failed in Maths or English or both
= 35 + 25 + 17 = 77
Pass % in both = 100 - 77 = 23%

questions.question: Find the sum of money which increases by {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex} of itself every year and amounts to ₹ 450 in 5 years. If calculated as per simple interest formula.
questions.explanation:
Reasoning: If sum be ₹ 100, in each year,
it increases by {tex}\frac{1}{10}{/tex} he., by ₹ 10; so the
interest is 10%. Amount is ₹ 450.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Principal = {tex}\frac{100 \times \mathrm{A}}{100+(\mathrm{r} \times \mathrm{t})}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{100 \times 450}{100+(5 \times 10)}{/tex} = ₹ 300.

questions.question: On a test containing 150 questions carrying 1 mark each, Rajnish answered 80% of first 75 questions correctly. What percent of the other 75 questions does he need to answer correctly to score 60% in the examination ?
questions.explanation:
Total marks to score {tex}\frac{150 \times 60}{100}{/tex} = 90
Marks obtained in first 75 questions
= {tex}\frac{75 \times 1 \times 80}{100}{/tex} = 60
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Marks to be obtained in next 75 questions
= 90 - 60 = 30
{tex}\therefore{/tex} % of questions to be answered correctly
= {tex}\left(\frac{30 \times 1 \times 100}{75}\right) {/tex} % = 40%

questions.question: The salaries of A, B and C together amount to ₹ 333, if they spend 80%, 85% and 75% of their respective salaries. If their savings are 7 : 6 : 9, then their respective salaries are
questions.explanation:
Let salaries of A, B, C be ₹ x, y, z espectively.
Together their amount = x + y + z = 333
Now,
Expenditure of A = 80%
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} Savings = 20%
Expenditure of B = 85%
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} Savings = 15%
Expenditure of C = 75%
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} Savings = 25%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Savings of A
= {tex}\frac{20 x}{100}, \mathrm{~B}=\frac{15 \mathrm{y}}{100}, \mathrm{C}=\frac{25 \mathrm{z}}{100}{/tex}
Also given savings are 7 : 6 : 9
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\frac{20 x}{700}=\frac{15 y}{600}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25 z}{900}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{20 x}{7}=\frac{15 y}{6}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{25 z}{9}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{20 x}{7}=\frac{15 y}{6}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 8x = 7y = x = {tex}\frac{7 y}{8}{/tex}
and {tex}\frac{15 y}{6}=\frac{25 z}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{9 y}{10}=z{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + y + z = 333
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{7}{8} y+y+\frac{9}{10} y{/tex} = 333
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 222y = (333)(80)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 120
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{7}{8}(120)=105, {/tex} {tex}z=\frac{9}{10}(120){/tex} = 108
Hence, salary of A = ₹ 105, B = ₹ 120, C = ₹ 108

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 300 and sold one out of four pens at no loss or profit. He sold the remaining pens at a profit of 20%. What is his overall profit or loss percent on the whole transaction?
questions.explanation:
Pen sold at no loss or no profit = {tex}\frac{300}{4}{/tex} = 75
Pens sold at 20% profit = 225
Let CP of one pen = ₹ 1
CP of 225 pens = ₹ 2255
SP of 225 pens = {tex}\left(\frac{100+20}{100}\right) \times 225{/tex} = {tex}\frac{120}{100} \times 225{/tex}
= ₹ 270
Total CP = ₹ 300
Total SP = 270 + 75 = ₹ 345
Profit % = {tex}\frac{45}{300} \times 100{/tex} = 15

questions.question: A candidate who gets 30% of the marks in an examination fails by 50 marks. Another candidate who gets 320 marks fails by 30 marks. Then the maximum marks is
questions.explanation:
Let the full marks = x
First candidates gets 30% of x in exam
i.e., he gets {tex}\frac{30x}{100}{/tex} marks in the exam
Now, another candidate gets 320 marks and fails by 30 marks
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Passing marks = 320 + 30 = 350
Now, first candidate fails by 50 marks
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex} \frac{30 x}{100}+50{/tex} = 350
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1000

questions.question: Rajni sold a watch at a profit of 15%. Had he bought it at 10% less and sold it for ₹ 28 less. He would have gained 20%. The C.P. of the watch is
questions.explanation:
Let CP = ₹ x
First SP = 115% of x = {tex}\frac{23}{20} x{/tex}
second CP = 90% of x = {tex}\frac{9 x}{10}{/tex}
Second SP = 120% of {tex}\frac{9 x}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{120}{100} \times \frac{9 x}{10}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{27 x}{25}{/tex}
It is given that
{tex}\frac{23 x}{20}-\frac{27 x}{25}{/tex} = 28
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{115 x-108 x}{100}{/tex} = 28
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} x=\frac{28 \times 100}{7}{/tex} = ₹ 400

questions.question: The cost price of an article increases by ₹ 100. The selling price increases by 10%. If the new profit decreases from 15% to 10%, what is the original cost price?
questions.explanation:
Let original cost price = x
S.P at profit of 15% = 1.15x
New cost price = ₹ (x + 100)
New S. P at profit of 10% = ₹ 101 (x + 100)
New S. P = 1.1 {tex}\times{/tex} original S.P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1.1 (x + 100) = 1.1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.15 x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ₹ 666.6

questions.question: What annual instalment will discharge a debt of ₹ 1092 due in 3 years at 12% simple?
questions.explanation:
Let each instalment = x
{tex}\left(x+\frac{x \times 12 \times 1}{100}\right)+\left(x+\frac{x \times 12 \times 2}{100}\right)+x{/tex} = 1092
= {tex}\frac{28 x}{25}+\frac{31 x}{25}+x{/tex} = 1092
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 325

questions.question: A shopkeeper sold two watches for ₹ 425 each, gaining 10% on one and losing 10% on the other. Then he
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{10 \times 10}{100}{/tex} = 1% loss.

questions.question: A man borrowed ₹ 24000 from two money lenders. For one loan he paid 15% annum and for the other 18% per annum. At the end of one year, he paid simple interest of ₹ 4050. How much did he borrow at each rate
questions.explanation:
Let sum borrowed at 15% = x
Sum borrowed at 18% = 24000 - x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{x \times 15 \times 1}{100}+\frac{(24000-x) \times 18 \times 1}{100}{/tex} = 4050
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 9000
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Money borrowed at 18% = 15000

questions.question: A sum was put at SI at a certain rate for 4 years. Had it been put at 2% higher rate, it would have fetched ₹ 56 more? Find the sum is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{P} \times(\mathrm{R}+2) \times 4}{100}-\frac{\mathrm{P} \times 4 \times \mathrm{R}}{100}{/tex} = 56
{tex}\frac{4 \mathrm{P} \times 2}{100}{/tex} = 56
P = ₹ 700.

questions.question: In an examination, a student who gets 20% of the maximum marks fails by 5 marks. Another student who gets 30% of maximum marks gets 20 marks more than the pass mark. The necessary percentage required for passing is
questions.explanation:
Let the maximum marks in the examination = x
According to question,
20% of x + 5 = 30 % of x - 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{x}{5}+5=\frac{3 x}{10}-20{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\frac{3 x}{10}-\frac{x}{5}{/tex} = 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{x}{10}{/tex} = 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 250
Passing marks = 20% of 250 + 5
= {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 250+5{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} % passing marks
= {tex}\left(\frac{55}{250} \times 100\right) \%{/tex} = 22%

questions.question: A sum of ₹ 731 is divided among A, B and C,  such that A receives 25% more than B and B receives 25% less than C. What is C’s share?
questions.explanation:
Let C share = ₹ x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} B share = {tex}x-\frac{25}{100} x=\frac{3 x}{4}{/tex}
A’s share = {tex}\frac{3}{4} x+25 \%{/tex} of {tex}\frac{3}{4} x{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 x}{4} \times \frac{125}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15 x}{16}{/tex}
Now, it is given that
{tex}\frac{15 x}{16}+\frac{3}{4} x+x{/tex} = 731
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x + 12x + 16x = 731 {tex}\times{/tex} 16
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 43x = 731 {tex}\times{/tex} 16
x {tex}=\frac{731 \times 16}{43}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 272
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C’ share = ₹ 272

questions.question: Gopi borrowed ₹ 1800 at 12% per annum for 2 years and Krishna borrowed ₹ 1200 at 18% per annum for 3 years. Then the ratio of interests paid by them is
questions.explanation:
Gopi Krishna
P = ₹ 1800 P = ₹ 1200
R= 12% R= 18%
T = 2 years T = 3 years
{tex}I_1=\frac{P T R}{100}{/tex} {tex}I_2=\frac{P T R}{100}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1800 \times 2 \times 12}{100}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1200 \times 3 \times 18}{100}{/tex}
= ₹ 432 = ₹ 648

I1: I2 = 432 : 648 = 2 : 3.

questions.question: If A’s salary is 50% more than B’s, then by what % B’s salary is less than A’s salary?
questions.explanation:
Let B’s salary be ₹ 100, so A’s salary is ₹ 150.
So % of B’s salary less than A
= {tex}\frac{50}{150} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}=33 \frac{1}{3} \%{/tex}

questions.question: Soha purchased an item for ₹ 9,600 and sold it for loss of 5 percent. From that money she purchased another item and sold it for gain of 5 percent. What is her overall gain/loss?
questions.explanation:
Cost price of an item = ₹ 9600
Loss = 5%
{tex}\text { S. P. }\left(\frac{100-\text { Loss\% }}{15}\right) \times \text { C.P. }{/tex}
S.P. {tex}\frac{95}{100} \times 9600{/tex}
S.P. = ₹ 9120
Now, C. P. of another item = ₹ 912
0 Gain = 5
S.P. {tex}\left(\frac{100+G}{100}\right) \times C . P{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{105}{100} \times 9120{/tex} = ₹ 9576
Overall loss = 9600 - 9576 = ₹ 24

questions.question: Mr. Pant purchased a car for ₹ 3,00,000 and a bike for his son for ₹ 1,00,000. He sold the car at a profit of 10% and bike at a loss of 20%. What is the net gain or loss?
questions.explanation:
C.P. of car = ₹ 3,00,000
Profit % of car = 10%
S.P. of car {tex}=\left(\frac{10+\text { Gains } \%}{100}\right) \times \text { C.P. }{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{110}{100}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 3,00,000
= ₹ 3,30,000
C.P. of bike = ₹ 1,00,000
Loss % of bike = 20%
S.P. of bike {tex}=\left(\frac{100-\operatorname{Loss} \%}{100}\right) \times \text { C.P. }{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{80}{100} \times{/tex} 1,00,000 = 80,000
His total C.P = ₹ 4,00,000
Total S.P = ₹ 4,10,000
Profit = 10,000
Profit% {tex}=\frac{10,000}{4,00,000} \times 100{/tex} = 2.5%

questions.question:
Which of the following is not true?

  1. {tex}3 \frac{3}{8}{/tex} = 3.375 = 337.5%
  2. {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} = 0.4 = 40%
  3. {tex}2 \frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 2.5 = 25%
  4. {tex}\frac{7}{10}{/tex} = 0.7 = 70%
questions.explanation:
{tex}3 \frac{3}{8}=-\frac{27}{8}={/tex} 3.375 = 337.5%
So, (a) is true.
{tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} = 0.4 = 40%. So (b) is true.
{tex}2\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} = 2.5 * 25%. So (c) is false
{tex}\frac{7}{70}{/tex}= 0.7 = 70%. So (d) is true.

questions.question: A man buys 5 apples for ₹ 3 and sold each for ₹ 2. what did he gain or lose?
questions.explanation:
C.P. = ₹ 3
S.P. = ₹ 10
Gain = 1 0 - 3 = 7
Gain % = {tex}\frac{7}{3} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{700}{3}{/tex}%

questions.question: If a commission of 10% is given on the written price, the gain is 50%. What shall be the percentage of gain, if the commission is increased to 25%?
questions.explanation:
Let M.P. = ₹ 100
Commission = ₹ 10
So, S.P. = ₹ 90
So, C.P. = {tex}\frac{100 \times 90}{150}{/tex} = ₹ 60
If commission is 25%, then S.P. = 75
So, gain% = {tex}\frac{15}{60} \times 100{/tex} = 25%
