= 102 + 1 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 + 1 = 100 + 1 = 101

questions.question: The standard form of the number 12345 is
questions.explanation:
A number in standard form is written as a {tex}\times{/tex} 10k, where a is a terminating decimal such that 1 {tex}\le{/tex} a {tex}\le{/tex} 10 and k is an integer.
So, 12345 = 1.2345 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

questions.question: Which of the following is equal to 1?
questions.explanation:
Let us solve all the expressions,
For option 20 + 30 + 40,
20 + 30 + 40 = 1 + 1 + 1 [{tex}\therefore{/tex} ao = 1]
= 3
For option 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 40,
20 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 [{tex}\therefore{/tex} ao = 1]
= 1
(30 – 20) {tex}\times{/tex} 40  = 0
(30 – 20) {tex}\times{/tex} (30 + 20) = 0
Hence 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 is the correct answer.

questions.question: In standard form, the number 72105.4 is written as 7.21054 {tex}\times{/tex} 10n where n is equal to
questions.explanation:
We know that, if the given number is greater than or equal to 10, then the power of 10 (i.e. n) is a positive integer equal to the number of places the decimal point has been shifted.
Hence, 72105.4 = 7.21054 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

questions.question: Square of {tex}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Square of {tex}\frac{-2}{3}{/tex} is {tex}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)^{2}{/tex},
So, {tex}\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)^{2}=\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right) \times\left(\frac{-2}{3}\right)=\frac{4}{9}{/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} multiplication of two rational numbers with same sign is always positive]

questions.question: Cube of {tex}\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Cube of {tex}\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right){/tex} is {tex}\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right)^{3}{/tex}
So, {tex}\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right)^{3}=\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{(-1) \times(-1) \times(-1)}{4 \times 4 \times 4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{64}{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following is not equal to 1?
questions.explanation:
Let us solve the expressions.
For option{tex}\frac{2^{3} \times 3^{2}}{4 \times 18}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{2^{3} \times 3^{2}}{4 \times 18}{/tex}  = {tex}\frac{2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 3}{4 \times 18}{/tex}  = {tex}\frac{4 \times 18}{4 \times 18}{/tex}  = 1
For option {tex}\left[(-2)^{3} \times(-2)^{4}\right] \div(-2)^{7}]{/tex}, {tex}\left[(-2)^{3} \times(-2)^{4}\right]+(-2)^{7}=\frac{(-2)^{3} \times(-2)^{4}}{(-2)^{7}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(-2)^{3+4}}{(-2)^{7}} {/tex} ...{tex}\left[\because a^{m} \times a^{n}=a^{m+n}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(-2)^{7}}{(-2)^{7}} {/tex} = 1 .....{tex}\left[\because \frac{a^{m}}{a^{n}}=a^{m-n}\right]{/tex}
For option {tex}\frac{3^{0} \times 5^{3}}{5 \times 25}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{3^{0} \times 5^{3}}{5 \times 25}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1 \times 5 \times 5 \times 5}{5 \times 25}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5 \times 25}{5 \times 25} {/tex} .....{tex}\left[\because a^{0}=1\right]{/tex}
= 1
For option {tex}\frac{2^{4}}{\left(7^{0}+3^{0}\right)^{3}}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{2^{4}}{\left(7^{0}+3^{0}\right)^{3}} = \frac{2^{4}}{(1+1)^{3}}{/tex} ...{tex}\left[\because a^{0}=1\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2^{4}}{2^{3}} = 2^{4-3}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \frac{a^{m}}{a^{n}}=a^{m-n}, m>n\right]{/tex}
= -21 = 2
Hence, option {tex}\frac{2^{4}}{\left(7^{0}+3^{0}\right)^{3}}{/tex} is not equal to 1.

subtopics.name: Dividing Powers with the Same Base (VII-11.2)

questions.question: {tex}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^3 \times\left(\frac{5}{7}\right)^3{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
We know that, if a, b and m are rational numbers, then
am {tex}\times{/tex} bm = (ab)m
So, {tex}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{3} \times\left(\frac{5}{7}\right)^{3}=\left(\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{5}{7}\right)^{3}{/tex}

questions.question: In standard form, the number 829030000 is written as K {tex}\times{/tex} 108 where K is equal to
questions.explanation:
We have,
A number in a standard form is written as K {tex}\times{/tex} 108, then K will be a terminating decimal such that 1 {tex}\le{/tex} K {tex}\le{/tex} 10.
So, there is only one option, where K = 8.2903 < 10

questions.question: In standard form, 72 crore is written as
questions.explanation:
We know that,
A number in standard form is written as a {tex}\times{/tex} 10k, where a is the terminating decimal such that 1 {tex}\le{/tex} a {tex}\le{/tex} 10 and k is an integer.
So, 72 crore = 720000000 = 7.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 108
Note Here, power of 10 (i.e. k) is a positive integer equal to the number of places the decimal point has been shifted.

questions.question: For non-zero numbers a and b, {tex}\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^m \cdot\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^n{/tex} where m > n, is equal to
questions.explanation:
We know that,
{tex}a^{m}+a^{n}=a^{m-n},(m>n){/tex}
So, {tex}\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^{m}+\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^{n}=\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^{m-n}{/tex}

questions.question: Which power of 8 is equal to 26?
questions.explanation:
Let power of 8 be x.
According to the question.
8x = 26 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (23)x = 26 [{tex}\because{/tex}8 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 23]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 23x = 26 {tex}\left[\because\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m \times n}\right]{/tex}
since base are equal, then by equating their exponents, we get
3x = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3 x}{3}=\frac{6}{3}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 3]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
Hence, the power of 8 is 2, which is equal to 26.

questions.question: Which of the following is not equal to {tex}\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right)^{4}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
We know that, {tex}\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)^{m}=\frac{p^{m}}{q^{m}}{/tex}
So, {tex}\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right)^{4}=\frac{(-5)^{4}}{(4)^{4}}{/tex} or {tex}\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right)^{4}=\frac{(5)^{4}}{(-4)^{4}}{/tex}
or {tex}\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right)^{4}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{-5}{4}\right){/tex}
Hence, options {tex}-\frac{5^{4}}{4^{4}}{/tex} is not equal to the given in the question.

questions.question: The simplified form of (-4)5 {tex}\div{/tex} (-4)8 is
questions.explanation:
We have, (-4)5 {tex}\div{/tex} (-4)8
{tex}=\frac{(-4)^5}{(-4)^8}=\frac{1}{(-4)^8 \times(-4)^{-5}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} am = {tex}\frac{1}{a^{-m}}{/tex}]
{tex}=\frac{1}{(-4)^{8-5}}=\frac{1}{(-4)^3}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} am {tex}\times{/tex} an = am+n]
which is the required form.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2^3}\right)^2{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
We have. {tex}\left(\frac{1}{2^3}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1)^2}{\left(2^3\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^m=\frac{a^m}{b^m}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2^6}=\frac{1}{64}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} (am)n = am{tex}\times{/tex}n]

questions.question: Evaluate (-3)4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^4{/tex}
questions.explanation:
We have, (-3)4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^4{/tex}
= (-1 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^4{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} -a = -1 {tex}\times{/tex} a]
= (-1)4 {tex}\times{/tex} 34 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{5^4}{3^4}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} (a {tex}\times{/tex} b)m = am {tex}\times{/tex} bm, {tex}\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)^m=\frac{a^m}{b^m}]{/tex}
= 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 = (5)4 [{tex}\because{/tex} (-1)4 = 1]
which is the required form.

questions.question: If 5a = 3125, then the value of 5a-3 is
questions.explanation:
Given, 5a = 3125
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5a = 55
On comparing, we get
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 5a-3 = 55-3 = 25

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left[\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{\frac{5}{2}}\right]^{\frac{3}{5}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{\frac{5}{2}}\right]^{\frac{3}{5}}=\left[\left\{\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^2\right\}^{\frac{5}{2}}\right]^{\frac{3}{5}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^5\right]^{\frac{3}{5}}=\left(\frac{5}{3}\right)^3=\frac{125}{27}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{125}\right)^{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(-\frac{1}{125}\right)^{\frac{-2}{3}}=\left[\left(-\frac{1}{5} \times-\frac{1}{5} \times-\frac{1}{5}\right)^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right]^2{/tex}
= (-5)2 = 25

questions.question: If {tex}9 \sqrt{x}=\sqrt{12}+\sqrt{147}{/tex}, then the value of x is
questions.explanation:
{tex}9 \sqrt{x}=\sqrt{12}+\sqrt{147}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 3}+\sqrt{3 \times 7 \times 7}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \sqrt{3}+7 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 9 \sqrt{x}=9 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{\frac{1}{2}}=3^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
On comparing both sides, we get x = 3

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2}}}}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2 \sqrt{2}}}}} =2^{\frac{z^4-1}{2^4}}{/tex}
= {tex}2^{\frac{34}{32}}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\frac{(\frac{3}{4})^5}{(\frac{5}{3})^5}{/tex} =
questions.explanation: {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\left( \frac{3}{4} \right)^5}{\left( \frac{5}{3} \right)^5}{/tex} = {tex}\left( \frac{\frac{3}{4}}{\frac{5}{3}} \right)^5{/tex}  [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a^m}{b^m} = \left( \frac{a}{b} \right)^m{/tex}]

questions.question: The value of {tex}5+\sqrt{11+\sqrt{19+\sqrt{29+\sqrt{49}}}}{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{5+\sqrt{11+\sqrt{19+\sqrt{29+\sqrt{49}}}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5+\sqrt{11+\sqrt{19+\sqrt{29+7}}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5+\sqrt{11+\sqrt{19+6}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5+\sqrt{11+5}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{5+4}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{9}{/tex}
= 3

questions.question: 25450 expressed in standard form is
questions.explanation: Standard form of 25450 = 2.5450 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

questions.question: If ax = by = cz and b2 = ac, then y equals
questions.explanation:
We have, ax = by = cz and b2 = ac
For ax = by {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}a^{\frac{x}{y}}=b^{\frac{y}{y}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = {tex}a^{\frac{x}{y}}{/tex} ...(i)
Also, cz = ax {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}c^{\frac{z}{z}}=a^{\frac{x}{z}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = {tex}a^{\frac{x}{z}}{/tex} ...(ii)
Now, b2 = ac
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(a^{\frac{x}{y}}\right)^2{/tex} = a {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}a^{\frac{x}{z}}{/tex} [From (i) and (ii)]
{tex}\Rightarrow a^{\frac{2 x}{ y}}=a^{((\frac{x}{z})+1)}{/tex}
On comparing, we get
{tex}\frac{2 x}{y}=\frac{x}{z}+1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x}{y}=\frac{x+z}{z} \Rightarrow y=\frac{2 x z}{x+z}{/tex}

questions.question: When 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 - 2000 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6 is expressed in standard form, we will get
questions.explanation:
We have, 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 - 2000 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 103 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 103-6
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3
= (5 - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3

subtopics.name: Multiplying Powers with the Same Exponents (VII-11.2)

questions.question: Express the product of 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 and 2.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 in the standard form.
questions.explanation:
1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
= 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 = 2.88 {tex}\times{/tex} 103

questions.question: Earth is 1275000000 km away from the Saturn. Express this distance in standard form.
questions.explanation: 1.275 {tex}\times{/tex} 109

questions.question: The cell of bacteria doubles itself after every 1 hour. How many cells will there be after 8 hours?
questions.explanation:
Let no. of cells at present be x.
No. of cells after 1 hour = 2x
No. of cells after 2 hours = 2(2x) = 4x = 22x
No. of cells after 3 hours = 2(4x) = 8x = 23x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} No. of cells after 8 hours = 28x

questions.question: A rectangular piece of land is to be sold off in smaller pieces. The total area of the land is 217 sq. miles. The pieces to be cut out are 162 sq. miles in size. How many smaller pieces of the land can be sold at the given size?
questions.explanation:
Required number of pieces
= {tex}\frac{\text { Total area of land }}{\text { Area of each piece }}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2^{17}}{16^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2^{17}}{\left(2^4\right)^2}{/tex} = 29

subtopics.name: Dividing Powers with the Same Exponents (VII-11.2)

questions.question: At the end of the 20th century, the world’s population was approximately 6.125 {tex}\times{/tex} 109. Express this population in usual form.
23 + 23 + 23 + 23 is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
We have, 23 + 23 + 23 + 23
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 23
= 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 23 = 25

questions.question: All the planets revolve around the Sun in elliptical orbits. Uranus’s farthest distance from the Sun is approximately 3.004 {tex}\times{/tex} 109 km and its closest distance is approximately 2.749 {tex}\times{/tex} 109 km. What is the average distance of Uranus from the Sun?
questions.explanation:
Farthest distance of Uranus from Sun = (3.004 {tex}\times{/tex} 109) km
Closest distance of Uranus from Sun = (2.749 {tex}\times{/tex} 109) km
Total distance = 3.004 {tex}\times{/tex} 109 + 2.749 {tex}\times{/tex} 109
= (5.753 {tex}\times{/tex} 109) km
Average distance = {tex}\frac{5.753 \times 10^9}{2}{/tex}
= (2.8765 {tex}\times{/tex} 109) km

questions.question: The standard form of the number 56789 is ________.
questions.explanation: 5.6789 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

questions.question: The value of (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) + (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) {tex}\times{/tex} (-8) is ________.
questions.explanation:
(-8)5 + (-8)5 = -2 {tex}\times{/tex} (8)5
= -2 {tex}\times{/tex} (2)15 = -(2)16 = -(4)8

questions.question: Standard form of 79403.75 is ________.
questions.explanation: Standard form of 79403.75 is 7.940375 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

= x2y2x5y3

questions.question: If {tex}\frac Pq=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 \div\left(\frac{6}{7}\right)^0{/tex} then the value of ({tex}\frac qp{/tex})3 is-
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{p}}{\mathrm{q}}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 \div\left(\frac{6}{7}\right)^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 \div 1\left[a^{\circ}=1\right]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{p}}{\mathrm{q}}=\frac{4}{9}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\left(\frac{64}{125}\right)^\frac{-2} { 3} \div \frac{1} {(\frac {256}{625})^{1/4}}+\left(\frac{(\sqrt{25})}{\sqrt[3]{64}}\right)^0{/tex} = ________
questions.explanation:

= {tex}\left(\sqrt[3]{\frac{64}{125}}\right)^{-2} \div \sqrt[4]{\frac{625}{256}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)^{-2} \div\left(\frac{5}{4}\right){/tex}+ (1) [Using (9)o = 1]
= {tex}\frac{25}{16} \div \frac{5}{4}+1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{25}{16} \times \frac{4}{5}+1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5}{4}+1=\frac{9}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{p}{q}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^3 \div\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3}{/tex} then value of {tex}\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)^{-10}{/tex} is

{tex}\therefore\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)^{-10}{/tex} = (1)-10 = 1

questions.question: The solution of {tex}3^{3 x-5}=\frac{1}{9^x}{/tex} is.

questions.question: {tex}(64)^{\frac{-2}{3}} \times\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^{-3}{/tex} equal to

= {tex}4^{-2} \times \frac{1}{4^{-3}}{/tex} = 4.

questions.question: If {tex}\left(\frac{x^m \times x^n}{x^p}\right)^q{/tex} = xk then what is the value of K?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{x^m \times x^n}{\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{p}}}\right)^q{/tex} = xk
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{x^{m+n}}{x^p}\right)^q{/tex}= xk [Using am {tex}\times{/tex} an = am+n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (xm+n-p)q = xk [Using {tex}\frac{a^m}{a^n}{/tex} = am-n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} xq(m+n-p) = xk [Using (am)n = amn]
K = (m + n - p)q

questions.question: Number of prime factors in {tex}(216)^{\frac{3}{5}} \times(2500)^{\frac{2}{5}} \times(300)^{\frac{1}{5}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}(216)^{\frac{3}{5}} \times(2500)^{\frac{2}{5}} \times(300)^{\frac{1}{5}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(2^3 \times 3^3\right)^{\frac{3}{5}} \times\left(2^2 \times 5^4\right)^{\frac{2}{5}} \times\left(2^2 \times 3 \times 5^2\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}{/tex}
= {tex}2^{\frac{9}{5}} \times 3^{\frac{9}{5}} \times 2^{\frac{4}{5}} \times 5^{\frac{8}{5}} \times 2^{\frac{2}{5}} \times 3^{\frac{1}{5}} \times 5^{\frac{2}{5}}{/tex}
= {tex}2^{\left(\frac{9}{5}+\frac{4}{5}+\frac{2}{5}\right)} \times 3^{\left(\frac{9}{5}+\frac{1}{5}\right)} \times 5^{\left(\frac{8}{5}+\frac{2}{5}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}2^{\frac{15}{5}} \times 3^{\frac{10}{5}} \times 5^{\frac{10}{5}}{/tex} = 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 52
{tex}\therefore{/tex} No. of prime factors = (3 + 2 + 2) = 7

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2^{n-1}}+y^{2^{n-1}}\right)\left(x^{2^{n-1}}-y^{2^{n-1}}\right)}{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{\left(x^{2^{n-1}}\right)^2-\left(y^{2^{n-1}}\right)^2}{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\left[x^{\left(2^{n-1} \times 2\right)}\right]-\left[y^{\left(2^{n-1} \times 2\right)}\right]}{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2^{n-1+1}}-y^{2^{n-1+1}}}{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{x^{2^n}-y^{2^n}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1

questions.question: The value of x, when {tex}3^x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{9}{/tex} is:

questions.question: A new born bear weights 4 kg. How many kilograms might a five year old bear weigh if its weight increases by the power of 2 in 5 years?

According to question,
Weight of five years old bear = 42
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 16 kg.

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}-1\right)\left(2^{\frac{3}{4}}+2^{\frac{1}{2}}+2^{\frac{1}{4}}+1\right){/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}-1\right)\left(2^{\frac{3}{4}}+2^{\frac{1}{2}}+2^{\frac{1}{4}}+1\right){/tex}
Let {tex}2^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex} = a. Then,
Given exp. = (a - 1) (a3 + a2 + a + 1)
= (a - 1) (a2 (a + 1) + 1(a + 1))
= (a - 1) (a + 1) (a2 + 1) = (a2 - 1) (a2 + 1)
= a4 - 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required value = {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)^4{/tex} - 1 = 2 - 1 = 1.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\sqrt[3]{2} \times 7 \sqrt[3]{6} \times 5 \sqrt[3]{18}{/tex} is

= {tex}[105] \times[6]^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}{/tex} = 105 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 630

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\left(6^{-1}-8^{-1}\right)^{-1}+\left(2^{-1}-3^{-1}\right)^{-1}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\left\{8^{-\frac{2}{3}}\right\}^{\frac{(24)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{2}}{/tex} is

questions.question: {tex}(36+64)^\frac{1}{2}+\left(1+4^\frac{3}{2}\right)^\frac{1 }{2}+\sqrt{20}+\sqrt{45}{/tex} equals
questions.explanation:

{tex}\Rightarrow 13+5 \sqrt{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}-1\right)\left(2^{\frac{3}{4}}+2^{\frac{1}{2}}+2^{\frac{1}{4}}+1\right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
Given expression is
{tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}-1\right)\left(2^{\frac{3}{4}}+2^{\frac{1}{2}}+2^{\frac{1}{4}}+1\right){/tex} can be written as
{tex}\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}-1\right)\left(2^{\frac{3}{4}}+2^{\frac{2}{4}}+2^{\frac{1}{4}}+1\right){/tex} which is in the form
(x - 1) (x3 + x2 + x + 1) where {tex}x=2^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1) [x2 (x + 1) + (x + 1)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1)[(x + 1)(x2 + 1)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x2 - 1) (x2 + 1) = x4 - 1
[Using identity (a - b) (a + b) = a2 - b2]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x^4-1=\left(2^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)^4{/tex} -1 = 2 -1 = 1.

questions.question: If {tex}x^{x^\frac{3}{2}}=\left(x^\frac{1}{2}\right)^x{/tex} then the value of x is ________.

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac 94{/tex}

questions.question: Solve for y if {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{1}{9}\right)^{2 y-1} \cdot(. 0081)^\frac {1}{3}}{\sqrt{243}}=\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2 y-5} \sqrt[3]{\frac{27^{y-1}}{10000}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:

{tex}\frac{\left(3^{-2}\right)^{2 y-1} \cdot\left(3^4 \cdot 10^{-4}\right)^\frac{1}{3}}{3^\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3^{-(2 y-5)} \cdot 3=3^{\left(\frac{y-1}{3}\right)}}{10^\frac{4}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3^{-4 y+2+\frac{4} {3}-\frac {5}{2}}}{10^\frac{4}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3^{-2 y+5+y-1}}{10^\frac{4}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow3^{-4 y+ \frac{5}{6}}=3^{-y+4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -4y + {tex}\frac 56{/tex} = - y + 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac{-19}{18}{/tex}

questions.question:
If x, y, z, are positive real number and a, b, c are rational numbers, then the value of
{tex}\frac{1}{1+x^{b-a}+x^{c-a}}+\frac{1}{1+x^{a-b}+x^{c-b}}{/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{1+x^{b-c}+x^{a-c}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{1+x^{b-a}+x^{c-a}}+\frac{1}{1+x^{a-b}+x^{c-b}}+\frac{1}{1+x^{b-c}+x^{a-c}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{x^b}{x^a}+\frac{x^c}{x^a}}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{x^a}{x^b}+\frac{x^c}{x^b}}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{x^b}{x^c}+\frac{x^a}{x^c}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{x^a}{x^a+x^b+x^c}+\frac{x^b}{x^b+x^a+x^c}+\frac{x^c}{x^c+x^b+x^a}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{x^a+x^b+x^c}{x^a+x^b+x^c}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Simplify {tex}\frac{(-2)^3 \times(-2)^7}{3 \times 4^6}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(-2)^3 \times(-2)^7}{3 \times 4^6}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(-2)^{3+7}}{3 \times\left(2^2\right)^6}\left\{a^m \times a^n=a^{m+n}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(-2)^{10}}{3 \times 2^{12}}\left\{\left(a^m\right)^{n=a^{m \times n}}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(-2)^{10}}{3 \times 2^{12}}=\frac{2^{10-12}}{3}\left\{a^{\mathrm{m}} \div a^n=a^{m-n},(-2)^{10}=2^{10}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^{-2}}{3}=\frac{1}{3 \times 2^2}=\frac{1}{12}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{2^{m+3} \times 3^{2 m-n} \times 5^{m+n+3} \times 6^{n+1}}{6^{m+1} \times 10^{n+3} \times 15^m}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2^{m+3} \times 3^{2 m-n} \times 5^{m+n+3} \times 2^{n+1} \times 3^{n+1}}{2^{m+1} \times 3^{m+1} \times 2^{n+3} \times 5^{n+3} \times 3^m \times 5^m}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2^{m+n+4} \times 3^{2 m+1} \times 5^{m+n+3}}{2^{m+n+4} \times 3^{2 m+1} \times 5^{m+n+3}}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: {tex}\left(\frac{x^a}{x^b}\right)^c \times\left(\frac{x^b}{x^c}\right)^a \times\left(\frac{x^c}{x^a}\right)^b{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{x^a}{x^b}\right)^c \times\left(\frac{x^b}{x^c}\right)^a \times\left(\frac{x^c}{x^a}\right)^b{/tex}
= xac-bc {tex}\times{/tex} xab-ac {tex}\times{/tex} xbc-ab
= xac - bc + ab - ac + be - ab
= x0 = 1.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(5)^{0.25} \times(125)^{0.25}}{(256)^{0.10} \times(256)^{0.15}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(5)^{0.25} \times(125)^{0.25}}{(256)^{0.10} \times(256)^{0.15}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(5)^{0.25} \times\left(5^3\right)^{0.25}}{\left(4^4\right)^{0.10} \times\left(4^4\right)^{0.15}}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\left[\left(\sqrt[n]{x^2}\right)^{n / 2}\right]^2{/tex} is equal to:

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{(0.6)^0-(0.1)^{-1}}{\left(\frac{3}{2^3}\right)^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^3+\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)^{-1}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(0.6)^0-(0.1)^{-1}}{\left(\frac{3}{2^3}\right)^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^3+\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)^{-1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1-\frac{1}{0.1}}{\frac{2^3}{3} \times \frac{3^3}{2^3}+(-3)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-10}{9-3}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-9}{6}=\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{6^n \times 2^{2 n} \times 3^{3 n}}{30^n \times 3^{2 n} \times 2^{3 n}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:

{tex}=\left(\frac{3}{10}\right)^n{/tex} = (0.3)n

questions.question: Find x so that (-5)x+1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-5)5 = (-5)7
questions.explanation:
(-5)x+1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-5)5 = (-5)7
(-5)x+1+5 = (-5)7 {am {tex}\times{/tex} an =am+n}
(-5)x+6 = (-5)7
On both sides, powers have the same base, so their exponents must be equal.
Therefore, x + 6 = 7
x = 7 - 6 = 1
x = 1

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{2^\frac{1}{2} \times 3^\frac{1}{3} \times 4^\frac{1}{4}}{10^\frac{-1}{5} \times 5^\frac{3}{ 5}} \div \frac{4^\frac{-2}{3} \times 5^\frac{-7}{5}}{4^\frac{-3}{5} \times 6^\frac{-1} {3}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2^\frac{1}{2} \times 3^\frac{1}{3} \times 4^\frac{1}{4}}{10^\frac{-1}{5} \times 5^\frac{3}{ 5}} \div \frac{4^\frac{-2}{3} \times 5^\frac{-7}{5}}{4^\frac{-3}{5} \times 6^\frac{-1} {3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^\frac{1}{2} \times 3^\frac{1}{3} \times 2^\frac{1}{2}}{2^\frac{-1}{5} \times 5^\frac{-1}{5} \times 5^\frac{3}{5}}+\frac{2^\frac{-4}{3} \times 5^\frac{-7}{5}}{2^\frac{-6}{5} \times 2^\frac{-1}{3} \times 3^\frac{-1}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^\frac{6 }{5} \times 3^\frac{1}{3}}{5^\frac{2}{5}} \div \frac{2^\frac{1}{5} \times 3^\frac{1}{3}}{5^\frac{7}{5}}{/tex}
{tex}=2^{\frac{6}{5}-\frac{1}{5}} \times 5^{\frac{7}{5}-\frac{2}{5}}{/tex} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 10.

questions.question: The value of {tex}\frac{6^{n+3}-32.6^{n+1}}{6^{n+2}-2.6^{n+1}}{/tex} is equal to
By simplifying {tex}\frac{4 r}{\left(r^2 \mathrm{~m}\right)^2}{/tex}, we get:

questions.question: Which of the following not equal to y6?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(y^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{12}{/tex}
{tex}=y^{\frac{1}{3} \times 12}=y^4 \neq y^6{/tex}
But {tex}\left(y^{\frac{2}{3}}\right)^9{/tex} {tex}=y^{\frac{2}{3} \times 9}{/tex} = y6
{tex}\left(\sqrt{y^6}\right)^2{/tex} = y6
{tex}\sqrt[3]{y^{18}}=\left(y^{18}\right)^\frac{1}{3}{/tex} = y6

questions.question: Evaluate {tex}\left(1^3+2^3+3^3+4^3\right)^\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(1^3+2^3+3^3+4^3\right)^\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=(1+8+27+64)^\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=(100)^\frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[(100)^\frac{1}{2}\right]^{-3}{/tex} =(10)-3

questions.question: (2d2e-1)3 {tex}\times\left(\frac{d^3}{e}\right)^{-2}{/tex} = ________
questions.explanation:
(2d2e-1)3 {tex}\times\left(\frac{d^3}{e}\right)^{-2}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(2 d^2 e^{-1}\right)^3 \times\left(\frac{e}{d^3}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}=8 d^6 \frac{1}{e^3} \times \frac{e^2}{d^6}{/tex}
= 8 (d6-6) (e2-3)
[Using {tex}\frac{a^m}{a^n}{/tex} = am-n]
{tex}=8 d^{\circ} e^{-1}=\frac{8}{e}{/tex}

questions.question: Evaluate: {tex}(0.000343)^{\frac{-3}{2}} \times(0.49)^{\frac{-3}{2}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}(0.000343)^{\frac{-3}{2}} \times(0.49)^{\frac{-3}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{343}{1000000}\right)^{\frac{-3}{2}} \times\left(\frac{49}{100}\right)^{\frac{-3}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{1000000}{343}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}} \times\left(\frac{100}{49}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{10^6}{7^3}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}} \times\left(\frac{10^2}{7^2}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{10^6 \times 10^2}{7^3 \times 7^2}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}=\left(\frac{10^8}{7^5}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}=\frac{10^{12}}{7^{\frac{15}{2}}}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\frac{(243)^{\frac{n}{5}} \times 3^{2 n+1}}{9^n \times 3^{n-1}}{/tex}= ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(243)^{\frac{n}{5}} \times 3^{2 n+1}}{9^n \times 3^{n-1}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left[(3)^5\right]^{\frac{n}{5}} \times 3^{2 n+1}}{\left(3^2\right)^n \times 3^{n-1}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3^n \times 3^{2 n+1}}{3^{2 n} \times 3^{n-1}}{/tex} [am {tex}\times{/tex} an = am+n]
= {tex}\frac{3^{3 n+1}}{3^{3 n-1}}{/tex}
= 33n+1-3n+1 {tex}\left[\frac{a^n}{a^m}=a^{n-m}\right]{/tex}
= 32 = 9

questions.question: If 4x - 4x-1 = 24, find the value of (2x)x
questions.explanation:
Given 4x - 4x-1 = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x - 4x {tex}\times{/tex} 4-1 = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow 4^x\left(1-\frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow 4^x \times \frac{3}{4}{/tex} = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 32 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22x = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
now, (2x)x {tex}=\left(2 \times \frac{5}{2}\right)^{\frac{5}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=(5)^\frac{5}{2}=\sqrt{5^5}=5^{1+\frac{3}{2}}=5 \times 5^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex}

questions.question: If m and n are whole numbers such that mn = 121, then the value of (m - 1)n+1 is
questions.explanation:
mn = 121
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (11)2 = 121
{tex}\therefore{/tex} m = 11 and n = 2
(m - 1)n+1 = (11 - 1)2+1
= (10)3 = 1000

questions.question: {tex}\left(\frac{x^a}{x^b}\right)^{\frac{1}{a b}} \times\left(\frac{x^b}{x^c}\right)^{\frac{1}{b c}} \times\left(\frac{x^c}{x^a}\right)^{\frac{1}{a c}}{/tex} = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(\frac{x^a}{x^b}\right)^{\frac{1}{a b}} \times\left(\frac{x^b}{x^c}\right)^{\frac{1}{b c}} \times\left(\frac{x^c}{x^a}\right)^{\frac{1}{a c}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(\text{x}^a\right)^{\frac{1}{a b}}}{\left(\text{x}^b\right)^{\frac{1}{a b}}} \times \frac{\left(\text{x}^b\right)^{\frac{1}{b c}}}{\left(\text{x}^c\right)^{\frac{1}{b c}}} \times \frac{\left(\text{x}^c\right)^{\frac{1}{a c}}}{\left(\text{x}^a\right)^{\frac{1}{a c}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{\frac{1}{b}}}{x^{\frac{1}{a}}} \times \frac{(\text{x})^{\frac{1}{b c}}}{(\text{x})^{\frac{1}{b c}}} \times \frac{(\text{x})^{\frac{1}{a}}}{(\text{x})^{\frac{1}{c}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\left(\mathrm{x}^{\frac{1}{a}}\right) \cdot(\mathrm{x})^{-\frac{1}{a}}\right]{/tex}{tex}\left[x^{\frac{1}{b}} \times x^{-\frac{1}{b}}\right]{/tex}{tex}\left[x^{\frac{1}{c}} \times x^{-\frac{1}{c}}\right]{/tex}
= (xo)(xo) (xo)
= 1

questions.question: If ax = {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{b}}, \mathrm{b}^y=\sqrt[3]{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{c}^2=\sqrt{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} then the value of XYZ is
questions.explanation:
ax = {tex}\sqrt{b}, b^y{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt[3]{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} and cz = {tex}\sqrt{a}{/tex}
a2x = b, b3y = c, c2z = a
(a2x)3y = c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a6xy)2z = a
a12xyz = a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} xzy = {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x^{\sqrt{x}}{/tex} = {tex}(\sqrt{x})^x{/tex} then x is
questions.explanation:
{tex}x^{\sqrt{x}}=(\sqrt{x})^x{/tex}
{tex}x^{(x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}=\left((x)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)^x{/tex}
{tex}x^{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}=x^{\frac{x}{2}} \Rightarrow \sqrt{x}=\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{x^2}{2 \times 2}{/tex} [Squaring both sides]
1 = {tex}\frac x 4{/tex}
x = 4

questions.question: On simplification {tex}\left[\frac{x^{\frac{a}{a-b}}}{\frac{a}{x^{\frac{a}{a+b}}} \div \frac{x^{\frac{b}{b}}}{x^{\frac{b}{b+a}}}}\right]^{a+b}{/tex} reduces to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{x^{\frac{a}{a-b}}}{x^{\frac{a}{a+b}}} \times \frac{x^{\frac{b}{b-a}}}{x^{\frac{b}{b+a}}}\right]^{a+b}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[x^{\frac{a}{a-b} a+b} \times x^{\frac{b}{b-a} b+a}\right]^{a+b}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[x^{\frac{a^2+a b-a^2+a b}{a^2-b^2}} \times x^{\frac{b^2+a b-b^2+a b}{b^2-a^2}}\right]^{a+b}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[x^{\frac{2 a b}{a^2-b^2}} \times x^{\frac{2 a b}{b^2-a^2}}\right]^{\frac{1}{a+b} \times a+b}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[x^{\frac{2 a b}{a-b}} \times x^{\frac{-2 a b}{a-b}}\right]^{\frac{1}{a+b} \times a+b}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[x^{\frac{2 a b-2 a b}{a-b}}\right]{/tex} = xo = 1

questions.question: if {tex}a^{\frac{1}{x}}=b^{\frac{1}{y}}=c^{\frac{1}{z}}{/tex} and abc = 1, then the value of x + y + z is
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}a^{\frac{1}{x}}=b^{\frac{1}{y}}=c^{\frac{1}{z}}{/tex} and abc = 1
Let {tex}a^{\frac{1}{x}}=b^{\frac{1}{y}}=c^{\frac{1}{z}}{/tex} = k
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = kx, b = ky, c = kz
{tex}\therefore{/tex} abc = kx {tex}\cdot{/tex}ky{tex}\cdot{/tex}kz = kx+y+z
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} kx + y + z = 1 [abc = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} kx + y + z = k0 [x0 = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y + z = 0

questions.question: If the mean of three numbers a, b and c is 3, then {tex}\sqrt[3]{\left(7^{a+b-c}\right)\left(7^{b+c-a}\right)\left(7^{c+a-b}\right)}{/tex} equals
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt[3]{\left(7^{a+b-c}\right)\left(7^{b+c-a}\right)\left(7^{c+a-b}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt[3]{\left(7^{a+b-c+b+c-a+c+a-b}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt[3]{\left(7^{a+b+c}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(7^{\frac{a+b+c}{3}}\right){/tex} = 73 {tex}\left[\because \frac{a+b+c}{3}=\text { mean }=3\right]{/tex}

questions.question: If f(x) = 2x0 + {tex}8 x^{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex} - 17x, then f(27) =
questions.explanation:
f(x) = 2xo + {tex}8 x^{-2}{/tex} - 17x
f(27) = 2(27)o + {tex}8(27)^{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex} - (1)7(27)
= 2(1) + {tex}8(\sqrt[3]{27})^{-2}{/tex} - (1)189 [Using 9o = 1]
= 2 + 8(3)-2 - (+1)
= 2 + {tex}\frac 89{/tex} + 1 = 18 + 8 - 9 = {tex}\frac{26-9}{9}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17}{9}{/tex}

questions.question: By what number should {tex}\left(\frac{-3}{2}\right)^{-3}{/tex}be divided, so that the quotient may be {tex}\left(\frac{4}{27}\right)^{-2}{/tex} ?
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{-2^7}{3^9}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}x=\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^{30} \div\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^{28}{/tex} and {tex}y=\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^2 \times\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^3{/tex} then find the value of (x3 {tex}\div{/tex} y)2.
questions.explanation:
{tex}x=\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^2{/tex}, {tex}y=\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^5{/tex}
{tex}x^3=\left(\frac{-2}{5}\right)^6{/tex}
{tex}x^3 \div y=\frac{-2}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (x3 {tex}\div{/tex} y)2 = {tex}\frac{4}{25}{/tex}

questions.question: Express {tex}\left[\left(2^{\frac{1}{2}} \cdot 4^{\frac{3}{4}} \cdot 8^{\frac{5}{6}} \cdot 16^{\frac{7}{8}} \cdot 32^{\frac{9}{10}}\right)^4\right]^{\frac{8}{25}}{/tex} as an integer.
Each of the numbers 1, 2, 3 and 4 is substituted, in some order for p, q, r and s. The greatest possible value of pq + rs is:
questions.explanation: pq + rs = (1)2 + (3)4 = 1 + 81 = 82

questions.question: If 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x = {tex}\frac{1}{512}{/tex}, then what is the value of {tex}\frac{-3}{x}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x + 4x = {tex}\frac{1}{512}{/tex}
{tex}8\left(4^x\right)=\frac{1}{512}{/tex}
{tex}4^x=\frac{1}{512 \times 8}{/tex}
4x = (512 {tex}\times{/tex} 8)-1
4x = 4-6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-3}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-3}{-6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac12{/tex} = 0.50

questions.question: Find the square root of: 4a2 + 9b2 + c2 - 12ab + 6bc - 4ac
questions.explanation:
4a2 + 9b2 + c2 - 12ab + 6bc - 4ac
= (-2a)2 + (3b)2 + c2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2a) {tex}\times{/tex} 3b
+ 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3b {tex}\times{/tex} c + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2a) {tex}\times{/tex} c
= (-2a + 3b + c)2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required square root = -2a + 3b + c.

questions.question: 10 + 103 equals ________
questions.explanation:
2x+1 = 8x
2x+1 = 23x
x + 1 = 3x
x = {tex}\frac12{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{(-2)^3 \times(-2)^7}{3 \times 4^6}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(-2)^3 \times(-2)^7}{3 \times 4^6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(-2)^{3+7}}{3 \times\left(2^2\right)^6}{/tex} {am {tex}\times{/tex} an = am+n}
= {tex}\frac{(-2)^{10}}{3 \times 2^{12}}{/tex} {(am)n = am{tex}\times{/tex}n}
= {tex}\frac{(-2)^{10}}{3 \times 2^{12}}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{2^{10-12}}{3}{/tex}{am {tex}\div{/tex} an = am-n, (-2)10 = 210}
= {tex}\frac{2^{-2}}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3 \times 2^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}.

questions.question: {tex}\left(2 d^2 e^{-1}\right)^3 \times\left(\frac{d^3}{e}\right)^{-2}{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left(2 d^2 e^{-1}\right)^3 \times\left(\frac{d^3}{e}\right)^{-2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(2 d^2 e^{-1}\right)^3 \times \frac{d^{-6}}{e^{-2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2^3 d^6 e^{-3}}{e^{-2}} \cdot d^{-6}{/tex} = = {tex}\frac{2^3 d^6 e^2}{d^6 \times e^3}{/tex} = 8e-1.

questions.question: {tex}\left\{\sqrt[4]{\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^\frac{-1}{2}}\right\}^\frac{-2}{3}{/tex} equals
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\sqrt[4]{\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2}}}\right]^{-\frac{2}{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{-2}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{1}{x}\right]^{+\frac{1}{12}}{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\sqrt{0.04 \times 0.4 \times a}{/tex} = 0.004 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{b}{/tex} then {tex}\frac ab{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt{0.04+0.4 \times a}{/tex} = 0.004 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{b}{/tex}
0.04 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} a = (0.004)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (0.4)2 {tex}\times{/tex} b
(Squaring both sides)
{tex}\frac ab{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(0.004)(0.004) \times(0.4) \times(0.4)}{(0.04) \times(0.4)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4 \times 4 \times 4 \times 10^{-7}}{4 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex} = 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5

questions.question: {tex}\frac{1}{1+a^{(n-m)}}+\frac{1}{1+a^{(m-n)}}{/tex} =?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{1+a^{n-m}}+\frac{1}{1+a^{m-n}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\frac{a^n}{a^m}}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{a^m}{a^n}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{a^m}{a^m+a^n}+\frac{a^n}{a^n+a^m}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a^m+a^n}{a^m+a^n}{/tex} = 1 {tex}\left[ \text { using } \frac{x^p}{x^q}=x^{p-q} \right]{/tex}

questions.question: Given that 9n + 9n + 9n = 32013, what is the value of n?
questions.explanation:
9n + 9n + 9n = 32013
3{tex}\cdot{/tex}9n = 32103
3{tex}\cdot{/tex}32n = 32013
3(1 + 2n) = 32013
1 + 2n = 2013
2n = 2013 - 1
n = {tex}\frac{2012}{2}{/tex} = 1006

questions.question: If 27k = {tex}\frac{9}{3^k}{/tex}, then value of {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{k}^2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
If 27k = {tex}\frac{9}{3^k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 33k = {tex}\frac{9}{3^k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 33k.3k = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 34k = 9 [am {tex}\times{/tex} an = am+n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 92k = 9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = {tex}\frac12{/tex} [am = an then m = n]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{k^2}{/tex} = 4

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{3^x}{1+3^x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac19{/tex}, the value of {tex}\frac{9^x}{1+9^x}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3^x}{1+3^x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 19{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x{tex}\cdot{/tex}9 = 1 + 3x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9{tex}\cdot{/tex}3x - 3x = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x(9 - 1) = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = {tex}\frac18{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = {tex}\frac{1}{64}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{9^x}{1+9^x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{64}}{1+\frac{1}{64}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac {\frac {1}{64}}{\frac {65}{64}}= \frac {1}{65}{/tex}

questions.question: The value of the expression {tex}\frac{(0.3)^\frac{1}{3} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{27}\right)^\frac{1}{4} \cdot(9)^\frac{1}{6} \cdot(0.81)^\frac{2}{3}}{(0.9)^\frac{3}{3} \cdot(3)^\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{-2} \cdot(243)^\frac{-1}{4}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(0.3)^\frac{1}{3} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{27}\right)^\frac{1}{4} \cdot(9)^\frac{1}{6} \cdot(0.81)^\frac{2}{3}}{(0.9)^\frac{3}{3} \cdot(3)^\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{-2} \cdot(243)^\frac{-1}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{3}{10}\right)^\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^\frac{3}{4}\left(3^2\right)^\frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{81}{100}\right)^\frac{2}{3}}{\left(\frac{9}{10}\right)^\frac{2}{3}(3)^\frac{-1}{ 2}\left(3^{-1}\right)^{-2}\left(3^5\right)^\frac{-1}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{10^\frac{-1}{3} 3^\frac{1}{3}(3)^\frac{-3}{4}(3)^\frac{1}{3}(3)^{4 \times \frac{2}{3}}\left(10^{-2}\right)^\frac{2}{3}}{3^\frac{4}{3} \times 10^\frac{-2}{3} \times 3^\frac{-1}{2} \times 3^2 \times 3^\frac{-5}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3^{\frac{1}{3}-\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{8}{3}} 10^{-\frac{4}{3}-\frac{1}{3}}}{3^{\frac{4}{3}-\frac{1}{2}+2-\frac{5}{4}} \times 10^{-\frac{2}{3}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(3)^{\frac{31}{12}} \cdot(10)^{-\frac{5}{3}}}{(3)^\frac{19}{12}(10)^{\frac{2}{3}}}{/tex}
= {tex}3^{\frac{31}{12}-\frac{19}{12}}10^{-\frac{5}{3}+ \frac 23}{/tex} = 31 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-1 = {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex} = 0.3

questions.question: Simplify: (4)3 + (3)2
questions.explanation:
(4)3 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 64 and (3)2 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 9
Now, (4)3 + (3)2 = 64 + 9 = 73

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)^4{/tex}.
questions.explanation: {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)^4{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{3}{4})\right.{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{3}{4}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \times 3 \times 3 \times 3}{4 \times 4 \times 4 \times 4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{81}{256}{/tex}

questions.question: By what number {tex}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{-5}{/tex} must be multiplies so that the result is {tex}\frac 2 3{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Required number = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \div\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{-5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (3)-5
= {tex}\frac{2}{3} \times\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^5{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{729}{/tex}

questions.question: Rajat created an informative message. He forwards the message to 5 people. Each of the five people forward the message to next 5 people and the chain goes on. Rajat did not forward the message. How many people have it?
The message is forwarded 5 times. Which of the following calculation can be used to find number of times message is forwarded?
questions.explanation: 50 + 51 + 52 + 53 + 54

questions.question: Which of the following represents the product of 1.08 {tex}\times{/tex} 1012 and 4.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 107?
questions.explanation: 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.0878 {tex}\times{/tex} 1019

questions.question: Which of the following shows the relation between mm and km?
questions.explanation: 1 mm = 10-6 km

questions.question: Number of lines of symmetry a triangle does not have:
questions.explanation: Triangle doesn't have 2 lines of symmetry, as a triangle can have 3, 1 or no line of symmetry, ie 3 in an equilateral triangle, 1 in an isosceles triangle and 0 in a scalene triangle.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetries are there in an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: 3 is correct as equilateral triangle is the one which characterized by three equal sides and opposite angle are 60 degree measure each. So equilateral triangle has three lines of symmetry that means when lines of symmetry passes through the mid point of each side of equilateral triangle it would divides the triangle into two equal parts.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetries are there in a rhombus?
questions.explanation: 2 is correct because the two lines passes through the corners of the rhombus and divide the rhombus in two equal parts.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetries are there in a square?
questions.explanation: 4 is correct because square has four equal sides that means the two lines passes through each diagonal and the other two lines cross horizontally and vertically through the middle of the square, these four lines of symmetry divides the square in two equal parts.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetries are there in regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: 5 is correct, because a regular pentagon has 5 sides so the number of lines of symmetry in regular pentagon are equal to the number of sides where each one goes form a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.question: Find the number of lines of symmetry in regular hexagon.
questions.explanation: As the regular hexagon has 6 equal sides, so there are 6 lines of symmetry to divides the regular hexagon in two equal parts.

questions.question: What is the order of rotational symmetry of the English alphabet Z?
State the number of lines of symmetry for a rhombus.
questions.explanation: As a rhombus has two lines of symmetry that run through one vertex to the one directly across it.

questions.question: In a complete turn (of 360o), the number of times an object looks exactly the same is called ________
questions.explanation: Rotational symmetry is the number times as object looks the same as in the initial position during the rotation at other points.

questions.question: A semi-circle has order of rotation ________.
questions.explanation: As when we rotate it from 0 to 360 degree, we get the exact image of semi-circle only once.

questions.question: A rectangle has order of rotation _________.
questions.explanation: As rectangle has two equal opposite side and all the angle of the rectangle are of 90 degree and it has two lines of symmetry so that when we rotate the rectangle it look twice.

questions.question: A rhombus has order of rotation ________.
questions.explanation: As rhombus has opposite angles are of equal measurement and the sum of the adjacent angles of the rhombus is 180o. Rhombus has a rotational symmetry of order 2, which means if the rhombus is rotated in the clockwise direction to complete one rotation.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetries are there in the regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular polygon is equal to the number of sides. A regular pentagon has 5 sides and 5 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Find the number of lines of symmetry in a regular hexagon.
questions.explanation: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular polygon is equal to the number of sides. A regular hexagon has 6 sides and 6 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: The order of the rotational symmetry of the parallelogram about the centre is ________.
questions.explanation: The same figure appears twice when rotated 360o around the centre.

questions.question: State the number of lines of symmetry for a regular hexagon.
questions.explanation: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular polygon is equal to the number of sides. A regular hexagon has 6 sides and 6 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: The angle by which the object rotates is called the ________.
questions.explanation: The angle at which shape or an object looks exactly the same during rotation is called the angle of rotation.

questions.question: In a complete turn (of 360o), the number of times an object looks exactly the same is called ________.
questions.explanation:
A shape has Rotational Symmetry when it still looks the same after rotation.
So, in a complete turn (of 360o), the number of times an object looks exactly the same is called rotational symmetry.

questions.question: If a figure has two or more lines of symmetry, should it have rotational symmetry of order more than 1?
questions.explanation: If a figure has two or more lines of symmetry, then it will definitely have its rotational symmetry of order more than 1.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry are there in a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: The number of lines of symmetry in a regular polygon is equal to the number of sides. A regular hexagon has 6 sides and 6 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: A rectangle has order of rotation ________.
questions.explanation: When rotated 360, the same image of the rectangle appears twice.

questions.question: A regular octagon has:

questions.question: The number of lines of symmetry of a square is_______.
questions.explanation: A square is symmetrical about its diagonals and the lines joining the mid points of its opposite sides.

questions.question: The order of rotational symmetry of an equilateral triangle is_______.
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry and it is rotated through 120o, 240o and 360o about the centroid 0.

questions.question: The number of lines of symmetry of an equilateral triangle is_______.
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has three lines of symmetry. This is because you can fold an equilateral triangle in half three different ways so that it matches up exactly with the opposite side. Folding a triangle at all three of its angles lets you see all three lines of symmetry.

questions.question: How many circular bases does a cylinder have?
questions.explanation: A cylinder is similar to a prism, but its two bases are circles, not polygons. Also the sides of a cylinder are curved, not flat. There are 2 circular bases 1 on top and 1 on bottom.

questions.question: The base of a prism is an octagon. Find the total number of edges on the prism.
questions.explanation:
An Octagon is a polynomial with 8 sides. In an octagonal prism, we have two octagonal faces so to 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 16 sides or edges.
And that eight corners of one octagon is additionally connected to 8 corners of other Octagon, form more 8 edges.
So total number of edges = 16 + 8 = 24

questions.question: How many vertices does a cone have?
questions.explanation: A cone has only one vertice.

questions.question: How many congruent edges does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 12 edges. Because all faces are squares and congruent to each other, all 12 edges are the same length. A point formed where edges meet. A cube has 8 vertices.

questions.question: How many bases does a pyramid have?

questions.question: What is the base of a hexagonal prism?
questions.explanation: A hexagonal prism is a prism with hexagonal base. It has 8 faces, 18 edges and 12 vertices.

questions.question: What is the base of a heptagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The heptagonal prism is formed by 7 square faces and two heptagons as base or cap. It has 14 vertices and 21 edges.

questions.question: What is the base of an octagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The octagonal prism is formed by sqaure sides and two regular octagon base or caps. So it's base is an octagon.

questions.question: What is the base of a pentagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The pentagonal prism is a prism with a pentagonal base. It has 7 faces,15 edges and 10 vertices.

questions.question: A pyramid with a square base has 5 faces and 8 edges. By Euler’s formula, find the vertices of the pyramid.
questions.explanation:
Euler’s formula: V + F - E = 2
where V, E, and F are respectively the numbers of vertices (corners), edges and faces in the given polyhedron
Here F = 5 and E = 8
So,  V = 2 + E - F = 2 + 8 - 5 = 5

questions.question: Find the number of edges in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation:
A rectangular pyramid has 4 side faces and one base. It has 5 vertices and 8 edges
So, the rectangular pyramid has 5 faces and 5 vertices.
Euler’s formula
F - E + V = 2
E = F + V - 2 = 5 + 5 - 2 = 8

questions.question: Find the number of vertices in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation:
A rectangular pyramid has 4 side faces which are triangles. The base is a square. It has 5 vertices and 8 edges.
Euler’s formula
F - E + V = 2
V = 2 + E - F
here E = 8 and F =5
So, V = 2 + 8 - 5 = 2 + 3 = 5

questions.question: Find the number of faces in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation: A rectangular pyramid has a rectangular base and 4 triangular faces. So total 4 + 1 = 5 faces

questions.question: How many edges are there in a cuboid?
questions.explanation: A cuboid is a polyhedron with six parallel faces. It has 8 vertices, 6 faces and 12 edges.

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Vertices = 12, Faces = 4, Edges =?
questions.explanation:
Formula: F + V - E = 2
Here Vertices = 12, Faces = 4 . i.e. V = 12 and F = 4
4 + 12 - E = 2
i.e. E = 4 + 12 - 2 = 14
So Edges = 14

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Faces = 5, Edges = 8, Vertices =?
questions.explanation:
Formula: F + V - E = 2
Here  Faces = 5, Edges = 8, So F = 5 and E = 8
So, 5 + V - 8 = 2
V = 2 - 5 + 8 = 5
So vertices = 5

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Edges = 2, Vertices = 3, Faces = ?
questions.explanation:
Formula is F - V + E = 2
So F = 2 + V - E
Here Edges = 2, Vertices = 3
So E = 2 and V = 3
F = 2 + V - E = 2 + 3 - 2 = 3
So 3 faces

questions.question: ___ + V - E = 2
questions.explanation:
Formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called Euler Formula
where V = number of vertices, E = number of edges, F= number of faces.

questions.question: The base of a prism is a pentagon. How many edges will the prism have?
questions.explanation:
The pentagonal prism is a prism with two pentagonal bases or faces which have 5 + 5 = 10 edges. And 5 edges are connecting these two bases.
So all total 10 + 5 = 15 edges are there.

An octagonal prism has 2 octagonal base or faces and 8 square faces. Two octagonal bases have 8 edges so total 16 edges and 8 edges are connecting them together.
So all total 16 + 8 = 24 edges.

questions.question: Which of the following can be calculated only for a cone but not for a cylinder?
questions.explanation:
The slant height of an object is the distance measured along its lateral face from the base to apex. The cylinder does not have any lateral face. So, the cylinder has no slant height.
But cone has slant height. The height of a cone from the vertex to the periphery is the slant height.

questions.question: In a solid, if F = V = 5, then the number of edges in this shape is?

Euler’s formula for any polyhedron is
F + V - E = 2
Given, F = V = 5
On putting the values of F and V in the Euler’s formula, we get
5+ 5 - E = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10 - E = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  E = 8

questions.question: We have 4 congruent equilateral triangles. What do we need more to make a pyramid?

2 squares with side length same as triangle.
2 equilateral triangles with side length same as triangle.

questions.explanation: We know that, a pyramid is a polyhedron, whose base is a polygon and lateral faces are triangles. But in the question, we have only 4 congruent equilateral triangles. Thus, we have to add a polygon in the base of atleast a four-sided figure. Hence, option (b) is required to make a pyramid.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be true for a polyhedron?
questions.explanation:
We know that, Euler’s formula for any polyhedron is F + V - E = 2
where, F = faces, V = vertices
and E = edges
For V = 4, F = 6 and E = 6
LHS = F +V - E
= 6 + 4 -6
= 1 0 - 6 = 4 {tex}\ne{/tex} RHS
Hence this can't be a polyhedron.

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 17 - E = 2

questions.explanation: It is clear from the figure that a cube has 6 faces.

questions.question: A polyhedron has 11 faces and 18 vertices. Find the number of edges.
questions.explanation:
Faces = 11
Vertices = 18
Using Eulers Formula,
F + V - E = 2
E = F + V - 2
= 11 + 18 - 2
= 29 - 2 = 27

questions.explanation:
9 faces.
(4 triangular face, 4 square faces and 1 square base)

questions.question: The side faces of a pyramid form its.
questions.explanation: lateral surface

questions.question: A solid has forty faces and sixty edges. Find the number of vertices of the solid.
questions.explanation:
For any polyhedron,
F + V - E = 2
Given,
F = 40
E = 60
V = ?
40 + V - 60 = 2
-20 + V = 2
V = 2 + 20
V = 22.

questions.question: If F = 4 and V = 3, then the value of E is (Use Euler’s formula):
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because{/tex} F = 4 and V = 3
We have Euler formula as:
F + V = E + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 + 3 = E + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} E = 5

questions.question: The end on which a prism may be supposed to stand is called ________ of the prism.
questions.explanation: base

questions.question: If F = n and E = n +1, then using Eulers formula, the value of V, is:
questions.explanation:
F = n
E = n + 1
Using Eulers Formula,
F + N - E = 2
V = E + 2 - F = n + 1 + 2 - n = 3

questions.question: A square pyramid is shown in the figure. Total number of edges, vertices and lateral faces are denoted by E, V and F. Find F + V - E.
questions.explanation:
A square pyramid has 5 faces, 8 edges and 5 vertices.
So, 5 + 5 - 8 = 2

questions.question: Find the number of vertices in a polyhedron which has 30 edges and 12 faces.
questions.explanation:
For any polyhedron,
F + V - E = 2
Here, F = 12, V = ?, E = 30
Using above formula,
12 + V - 30 = 2
V - 18 = 2
V = 2 + 18
V = 20
